Summary: Invalid hyperlinks to Simulink blocks in Model Advisor HTML reports generated with ModelAdvisor.run()
Description: If multiple models are opened and you use the ModelAdvisor.run()  API to run the Model Advisor, the generated Model Advisor HTML report contains invalid hyperlinks. Clicking on hyperlinks to Simulink blocks results in the following error:  The model is closed. Open the model to use the link . Since the model is opened, the error message is invalid. ?
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabrootat the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to <MATLAB> as in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of the applicable release folder: <MATLAB>/toolbox/simulink/simulink/modeladvisor/private.Delete the indicated files by following the steps below. cd to the folder: <MATLAB>/toolbox/simulink/simulink/modeladvisor/private.delete hiliteSystem.p;Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*:Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the folder. If prompted, click yes to for permission to replace the files.*Notes for Windows Systems:You need administrative privileges to save the .zipfile in the folder. To save the .zip file in the folder, open this EBR in a browser started in administrator mode.If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting Run as administrator.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zipfile.Extract the contents of the file to your folder.

Summary: Simulink.BlockDiagram.copyContentsToSubSystem might produce duplicate Simulink identifiers
Description: When you use the function Simulink.BlockDiagram.copyContentsToSubSystem  to copy the contents of a block diagram to a subsystem, multiple blocks might be assigned the same Simulink identifier.  This behavior occurs when both of the following conditions exist: The block diagram contains a link to a library. This library is not fully loaded when you call the Simulink.BlockDiagram.copyContentsToSubSystem  function.  This can result in incorrect behavior when tracing from the generated code to the model.
Workaround: Load the library using load_system before calling Simulink.BlockDiagram.copyContentsToSubSystem.

Summary: Dismissing a Simulink or Stateflow dialog boxe can cause a segmentation violation in GLObject::isObjectValid
Description: Dismissing dialog boxes in Simulink or Stateflow can crash MATLAB. The stack trace points to GLObject::isObjectValid .

Summary: First-generation blocks ignore parameter changes after entering invalid expression
Description: SimMechanics First Generation blocks may ignore changes to dialog box parameters. This error occurs when you enter an invalid expression in a dialog box parameter field and click Apply /OK . When this condition is met, the block ignores all subsequent attempts to correct the invalid expression. Consider an example in which you enter [1  in the Mass  field of a Body block dialog box and click Apply /OK . Because [1  is invalid, SimMechanics issues an error stating it cannot evaluate that expression. Correcting the expression (e.g. to 1)  and clicking Apply /OK  causes SimMechanics to reissue the previous error message.
Workaround: Use the set_param command to correct the invalid expression. The block begins to work as expected. For subsequent parameter changes you can use the dialog box directly. For more information, see the Simulink documentation for set_param.Alternatively, download and install a fix for this problem according to the following installation instructions.Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting Run as administrator.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: USRP(R) support package version 3.0.0 installer errors out if Simulink is not installed
Description: USRP(R) support package version 3.0.0 installer errors out with the following error message: Undefined function or variable 'sl_refresh_customizations'.  Error in /local/esozer/work/USRP/install_utils.p>install_utils (line 157)  Error in install (line 24)  install_utils(dataFile, 'install');  ? This error occurs if Simulink is not installed or a Simulink license is not available.
Workaround: Version 3.0.1 of the "MATLAB and Simulink Support Package for USRP? Radio", which was released on 7/2/2012, fixes this problem. You can download this version of the support package using the following link: https://www.mathworks.com/discovery/sdr/usrp.html

Summary: MATLAB terminates abruptly with Set Attribute or Get Attribute block with blank attribute value entered in dialog.
Description: In a Set Attribute block dialog, if the Value  field for any row is made blank and then the Value From  field for that row is changed to Signal port , then MATLAB may terminate abruptly upon compiling the model.In a Get Attribute block dialog, if the Default Value  field for any row is made blank and then the When Attribute Is Missing  field for that row is changed to Error , then MATLAB may crash upon compiling the model.
Workaround: To work around this issue, update the block dialog to not have any blank fields in the attribute table even if they are unused.

Summary: The Back EMF flat top area parameter of the Permanent Magnet Synchronous Machine block is inacessible (grayed out)
Description: The Back EMF flat top area  parameter of the Permanent Magnet Synchronous Machine block is not editable when the Back EMF waverform  parameter is set to Trapezoidal .
Workaround: To work around this issue:1. Open powerlib.2. Unlock the library.3. Type the following code at the command line: MV = get_param('powerlib/Machines/Permanent Magnet Synchronous Machine','MaskEnables'); MV{15} = 'on'; set_param('powerlib/Machines/Permanent Magnet Synchronous Machine','MaskEnables',MV); close_system('powerlib',1);

Summary: ga and gamultiobj sometimes violate linear constraints even when using the mutationadaptfeasible mutation function.
Description: When the active linear constraints are not linearly independent at the point that is being mutated, the mutationadaptfeasible  mutation function erroneously sets the new point to zeros. This can cause the population to be partly infeasible.

Summary: The interp1 function does not enforce error checks for infinite sample coordinates
Description: The interp1  function does not check for infinite sample coordinates and it handles Inf and -Inf sample coordinates inconsistently. The following example demonstrates the problem: X = [-Inf 2 3 4 5 Inf];V = 1:6;vq = interp1(X,V, 1)vq =   NaNvq = interp1(X,V,6)vq =     5 The interp1  function should check the sample coordinates for infinite values and raise an error.
Workaround: You can avoid this problem by removing the infinte coordinates and corresponding values from your data set.X = [-Inf 2 3 4 5 Inf];V = 1:6;idx = isinf(X);X(idx) = [];V(idx) = [];If your goal is to extrapolate the end-point values, you can introduce additional data points instead of using infinite coordinates. For example, the following code shows how to extrapolate beyond X=2 and X=5.X = [1 2 3 4 5 6];V = [2 2:5 5 ];vq = interp1(X,V, 1)vq =     2vq = interp1(X,V,6)vq =     5

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation for blocks connected to variant or function-call subsystem
Description: The C code generated from a Simulink model might be incorrect under all of the following conditions: The model contains a variant subsystem or function-call subsystem that is specified as inlined  (or auto , but is inlined in the generated code). For the function-call subsystem, it must be called from a user-written S-Function block. The variant or function-call subsystem contains an Inport block that is directly connected to an Outport block. In this case, the Inport block is non-virtual, which is indicated by an execution number. To see the execution number, on the model diagram window, enable Format > Block Displays > Sorted Order .  The generated C code might not include the code for the output signal of the Inport block.
Workaround: To avoid nonvirtual subsystem input ports, insert a Signal Conversion block at the output of the Inport block. This change does not affect the efficiency of the generated C code.

Summary: Incorrect function-call connection error when using Signal-Based Function-Call Generator block in SimEvents 4.0 (R2011b)
Description: When you compile a model containing a Signal-Based Function-Call Generator block that accepts a function-call input and delays it by a non-zero duration, the software might incorrectly issue an error message with the following information. "Function-call port of block 'myModel/Signal-Based Function-Call Generator' is connected to 'myModel/Signal-Based Function Call Generator' which does not produce a function-call signal. This error message is related to a hidden FunctionCallLoopBreak block."  This error message incorrectly indicates that the Signal-Based Function-Call Generator block is connected to itself, when it is not.
Workaround: This error can be safely ignored and is resolved when you re-compile the model.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated functions SetDims or FinalizeDims might access NULL pointers for referenced model with variable dimension signal
Description: When a model has a variable dimension signal, the generated SetDims  or FinalizeDims  functions might access NULL  pointers when all of the following conditions are true:  The model is a referenced model. The model parameter Total number of instances allowed per top model  is set to One .  The referenced model contains a subsystem, where the subsystem parameter Function packaging  is set to Function .  ?
Workaround: To work around this issue, do either of the following:Set the model parameter Total number of instances allowed per top model to Multiple.Change the subsystem whose Function packaging parameter is set to Function packaging to either Reusable function or Inline. ?

Summary: Code generation of a model might crash MATLAB
Description: Generating code for a model might crash MATLAB under the following conditions: The model contains a Stateflow chart or MATLAB block that uses local data with an initialization value The value of the model configuration parameter Maximum stack size (bytes) , on the Optimization > Signals and Parameters  pane, is set too low.
Workaround: Open the Configuration Parameters dialog box and on the Optimization > Signals and Parameters pane increase the value of the Maximum stack size (bytes) parameter.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Models that reference other models that contain either Sample Time Math blocks or masked blocks that contain SampleTime Math blocks can produce incorrect simulation results
Description: If a model that contains either a Sample Time Math block or a masked block that contains a Sample Time Math block (e.g. the Discrete Derivative block) is referenced by another model, then the software hard-codes the sample time of the Sample Time Math block into the generated code of the referenced model. If you reference this model when the 'Model' block of the parent model has a sample time that is different from that of the Sample Time Math block in the referenced model, then the simulation produces incorrect results.

Summary: graydist produces incorrect results
Description: graydist  contains a bug in the computation of the gray weighted distance transform due to faulty pseudo-code in the cited reference. As a result, calls to function graydist  produce incorrect answers for nearly all inputs (output is correct only for an image containing pixels of uniform intensity). For each output pixel, the gray-weighted distance should depend on the average pixel intensities of the previous pixel in the path and the pixel at which you arrive. In the initial implementation, the intensity of the previous pixel in the path is not used in the computation. This leads to problems in the computation of least cost gray-weighted paths. As an example, take the 'cityblock' gray-weighted distance calculation from the upper-right corner of the magic(3) matrix. A = magic(3);A = 8 1 6     3 5 7     4 9 2  The version containing the bug yields an incorrect result: graydist(A,1,1,'cityblock') ans =     0     1     7     3     6    13     7    15    15 The correct answer is: ans =         0    4.5000    8.0000    5.5000    7.5000   13.5000    9.0000   14.5000   18.0000
Workaround: There is no work around for this bug.

Summary: Simulink may not detect global data stores accessed during asynchronous tasks
Description: Model compilation should fail when a referenced submodel reads from or writes to a global data store during an asynchrnous task. However, Simulink may not detect such conditions in the following cases. The submodel reading from or writing to the data store is sample-time independent The submodel is itself placed inside a subsystem that is part of a top model that has an aperiodic rate

Summary: Generated code of a referenced model might crash due to uninitialized pointers
Description: When generating code for a referenced model, the generated code might access NULL  pointers when all of the following conditions are true: The model is a referenced model. The configuration parameter Total number of instances allowed per top model  is set to One . The referenced model contains a subsystem, where the subsystem parameter Function packaging  is set to Function . The referenced model contains a conditionally executed system.  ? When a crash happens, it can happen either when running a simulation of the top model with the referenced model being simulated using the Accelerator  mode, or when running the generated executable for the top model as a standalone application.
Workaround: To work around this issue, do either of the following:On the Model Referencing pane, set the configuration parameter Total number of instances allowed per top model to Multiple.Change all subsystems whose Function packaging parameter is set to Function packaging to Reusable function.?

Summary: Code generation causes assertion in cyclic flow charts
Description: The following circumstances can cause a crash at code generation time:A flow chart has cycles. There are multiple entries into the cyclic region. See the model sf_multiple_entries_into_loop_region.mdl  in the attached zip file sf_multiple_entries_into_loop_region.zip . Either of the following conditions is met:At least one of the transitions involved in the cyclic region has only comments in the label. The option Simulink block / Stateflow object comments  is checked in the Code Generation > Comments  section of model configuration parameters dialog.     An example crash has a stack trace including: cgir_xform.dll!CG::ConvertCyclicUnstructuredRegions::patchBackEdge(CG::LstLoopInterface<CG::Stmt,CG::Edge,CG::CfgAdaptorInterface> * aLoop=0x00000000c444ece0, CG::InPort loopHeadBackInPort={...})  Line 1005C++
Workaround: In order to workaround this, Do not use comments only in labels for transitions involved in a flow chart cycle.Turn off the option Simulink block / Stateflow object comments in the Code Generation > Comments section of model configuration parameters dialog.

Select Polyspace Verification , which opens a new window. Select option Use existing main . Start a verification.  Now, if you try to repeat step 3, a new window will not open and you will not be able to start a new verification.

To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux Systems: Type the following in a shell:unzip -l <POLYSPACE>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. See manual installation of Visual Studio plug-in in documentation.Reopen the Visual Studio environment.

Summary: Assigning int64 or uint64 variable into int property of FI object with WordLength of 32 bits or less crashes MATLAB
Description: If you assign an int64 or uint64 variable into the int  property of a FI object with a WordLength  of 32 bits or less, MATLAB crashes. An example of code that would cause such a crash is F = fi(0,0,16,0)   % 16 bit FI objectU = uint64(1)      % 64 bit integer valueF.int = U ?
Workaround: Use an int32, uint32, int16, uint16, int8, or uint8 variable when assigning into the int property of a FI object with a WordLength of 32 bits or less.

Summary: Callback actions on Stateflow annotations sometimes cause crashes
Description: Stateflow might become unstable if after you trigger a callback from an annotation, the callback action takes focus away from the Stateflow Editor and then you close the editor without focusing on a Stateflow chart. The symptom is a stack trace containing the line: failed assertion in .\utils\object.cpp at line 2853: object[id]!=NULL

Description: When running MISRA-C:2004 and DO-178B Model Advisor checks on a model with a PolySpace configuration component, the following error message occurs: Abnormal exit: Reference to non-existent field 'Tag'

Summary: Opening the dialog box of a Bus Selector block that is connected to masked Subsystem block causes MATLAB to crash
Description: If the input of the Bus Selector block is connected to output of a masked Subsystem block, then opening a Bus Selector dialog box causes MATLAB to crash.
Workaround: Open the masked Subsystem block (look under the mask).Open the Bus Selector block dialog box.

Summary: When simulated in Rapid Accelerator mode or Rsim target, a model with more than one variable sample time and a fixed sample time might incorrectly return extra time steps
Description: When simulated in Rapid Accelerator mode or the Rsim target, a model with more than one variable sample time and a fixed sample time might incorrectly return extra time steps. In cases where you expect to see a single sample time, you might see multiple sample times that are very close together (with the distance between them being on the order of your machine precision). ?

Summary: MATLAB stops responding when you start a software clocked acquisition with an MCC USB Device
Description: MATLAB stops responding with a segment violation when you start software clocked data acquisition with a Measurement Computing USB Device, using the legacy data acquisition adaptor. The issue occurs when you acquire analog data with the 'ClockSource' property set to 'Software'. ai = analoginput('mcc',0); cha=addchannel(ai,0); set(ai, 'ClockSource', 'Software'); start(ai); This results in a segmentation violation with this stack trace: [ 0] 0x77c29b2b C:\Windows\system32\msvcrt.dll+00039723 ( memcpy+000107 ) [ 1] 0x77e04733 C:\Windows\system32\OLEAUT32.dll+00018227 ( SysStringByteLen+000029 ) ...  This issue occurs when you use USB-1408FS and USB-1608FS from MCC.
Workaround: Use hardware clocking built into the device to avoid USB latency.

Summary: Incorrect current folder after opening a Simulink Project with startup shortcuts that set the current folder
Description: If you configure startup shortcuts to set the current folder, they are sometimes ignored by the Simulink Project Tool.A race condition can occur when using startup shortcuts that set the current folder, because there is a project setting on by default that sets the current folder to the project root. When there is a conflict between these settings, you might not see the current folder you expected. Startup shortcuts that cd into a folder can be overridden by the check box at the project node "Change current folder to project root on start up".The check box setting is on by default and incorrectly overrides the shortcut, unless the startup script takes a long time to run. The current folder will be the project root, unless the startup script is time-consuming, then the current folder will be the folder specified in the startup script. Two different results are possible due to a race condition in multithreaded code. The last code to run sets the working folder.If you have configured a startup shortcut to set the current folder, your setting should take precedence. In R2011b, the behavior is not always correct.
Workaround: To ensure the startup shortcuts can set the current folder, clear the check box "Change current folder to project root on start up" at the Project node in the Simulink Project Tool.

Description: After submitting a large project to the Polyspace Metrics database, you see the following error message when you try to update the database. Error: No response received after 10 minutes . You also see the following error in the polyspaced.log  file located in %POLYSPACE_COMMON%\..\Polyspace_RLDatas  for Windows or /var/Polyspace  for Linux: Error: Inconsistency between the uploaded comments and the repository results. Summary: Using Simulink.Bus.createObject with MATLAB structures containing arrays of substructures generates incorrect bus object
Description: When you call Simulink.Bus.createObject  using a MATLAB structure that contains one of more fields that in turn are arrays of MATLAB structures, the resulting bus objects) created in the base workspace do not have the correct dimensions at the elements that correspond to the fields that have an array of substructures in the input structure.
Workaround: After running Simulink.Bus.createObject, manually update the dimensions of the bus elements with the size of the corresponding (array of substructure) fields in the input structure. Either use the Bus Editor or update the dimensions property from the MATLAB command prompt using the Simulink.BusElement API.

Summary: SVN 1.7 is partially incompatible with Simulink Projects
Description: Consider the case where you use SVN 1.7 with Simulink Projects. If you create a project that is not at the root level of the sandbox: Simulink Projects will not detect that the project is being managed by SVN. Source control operations will not be available.  You will see an option to add the file to source control. However, if you try to use this option, the software fails. This bug was caused by a change in the way SVN sandboxes are structured.
Workaround: To workaround the problem:With SVN 1.7, create a Simulink Project in the same folder as the SVN sandbox root folder. This folder contains a hidden folder called .svn.Use SVN 1.6.

Summary: Simulink "Conditional input branch execution" optimization produces wrong answers for subsystem with constant sample time blocks
Description: Simulink produces wrong answers and generates wrong code if a model meets all  of the following conditions: The Configuration Parameters > Optimization > Conditional input branch execution  setting is enabled. The model contains a Switch block. A subsystem containing constant (Inf ) sample time blocks is directly connected to one of the data input ports (input port 1 and 3) of a Switch block. The blocks inside the subsystem are conditionally executed by the Switch block. You can identify such blocks by displaying the sorted execution orders (turn on Format > Block Displays > Sorted Order ). For example, if the Switch block displays as 2:5{3,4}  and the subsystem displays as 3:0{6} , 3:0  means that the execution order of the subsystem is first inside the conditional execution context {3}  set up by the Switch block input. Inside the subsystem, there is a constant sample time block and a virtual subsystem block, and the constant sample time block feeds into another constant sample time block inside the virtual subsystem.
Workaround: Clear the Configuration Parameters > Optimization > Conditional input branch execution parameter.

Summary: Incorrect number of Stateflow objects returned by sldiagnostics if a specified subsystem path has carriage-return characters
Description: sldiagnostics  returns an incorrect number of Stateflow objects if you specify a subsystem path as an input to sldiagnostics  and  this subsystem path contains carriage-return characters.
Workaround: In the call to sldiagnostics, replace all carriage-return characters in Simulink subsystem paths with space characters.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: n-D Lookup Table block: code generation and simulation results might not match, or code generation might error out when index search method is "evenly spaced points"
Description: Suppose that your model contains an n-D Lookup Table block, where all of the following conditions are satisfied: Inline parameters is "on". Index search method is "evenly spaced points". Breakpoint data type is integer or fixed point. The corresponding input port data type is floating point. The breakpoints have power-of-2 even spacing.  If the power is zero, code generation and simulation results do not match. If the power is greater than zero, code generation errors out.
Workaround: Try one of the following workarounds:In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, you can go to the Optimization pane and clear the Inline parameters check box.You can set the breakpoint data type to be the same as the corresponding input port data type.

Summary: For a model containing a Model block, Simulink may issue an error about inconsistent start time even though the start times of the top model and the referenced model are the same
Description: Simulink may issue an error for a model that meets all  of the following conditions The model contains a Model block. The start times for the top model and referenced model are non-zero. The referenced model is inheriting a sample time.  The error message is similar to this: Error in Model block 'top/A': the 'Start time' must match between the parent model 'top' and the referenced model 'ref'. The parent model has a value of '1', while the referenced model has a value of '2'. To change this parameter, go to the 'Solver' page of the Configuration Parameters dialog. This can happen because Simulink adjusts the start time of the all models to be an integer multiple of the fixed-step size.

Summary: save -append and growing a variable in a MatFile object does not compress the variable in a V7.3 MAT-file
Description: If you have a V7.3 MAT-file and you add a variable using save foo var -v7.3 -append  the variable is not compressed. Consequently, the file size increases more than expected. For example: var = ones(1000); save foo var -v7.3 d=dir('foo.mat');d.bytes save foo var -v7.3 -append d=dir('foo.mat'); d.bytes The output of d.bytes  indicates that the file size is significantly larger when the amount of data in variable var  is doubled. This is because the variable is not compressed. The same result occurs if you grow a variable using the MatFile  object. For example: mObj = matfile('foo', 'Writable', true);oneVar = ones(1,1000,1000); mObj.var(1,1:1000,1:1000) = oneVar; d=dir('foo.mat'); d.bytes mObj.var(2,1:1000,1:1000) = oneVar; d=dir('foo.mat'); d.bytes

Summary: Error: Unknown target: mcpu... (Advanced) when custom target is specified
Description: If you try to specify a custom target by selecting the option mcpu...(Advanced)  from the drop-down list of Analysis options > Target/Compilation > Target processor type , the verification fails to use this option and generates the following error message:  Error: Unknown target: mcpu... (Advanced)

Summary: In Simulink Debugger, when stepping into block methods, the displayed block port value is one step behind
Description: In the Simulink Debugger, when each block method is stepped into, the displayed block port value is one step behind. However, the input data for the subsequent block is displayed correctly.

Summary: Progress monitoring appears to stop after compilation when using option -to other
Description: When you run a verification on the Polyspace server with the option -to other , progress monitoring appears to stop after the compilation phase of the verification. However, this is not the case. See Workaround.
Workaround: Use the Polyspace Queue Manager to follow the progress of your verification.To view a log file on the server:Double-click the Polyspace Spooler icon, which opens the Polyspace Queue Manager Interface.Right-click the job you want to monitor.Select View log file. A window opens displaying the last one-hundred lines of the verification.?

Summary: Incorrect parameter setting for n-D Lookup Table block causes error during code generation instead of compile time
Description: Suppose that you have a model with an n-D Lookup Table block. When Extrapolation method  is Linear , the block input, output, and fraction data types must be floating point. If these data types are all homogeneous fixed point, an error should appear at compile time. However, the model compiles fine and the error does not appear until code generation.

Summary: Model configured for AUTOSAR code generation cannot use a custom storage class that is a reference to PerInstanceMemory
Description: Update diagram fails for a model that is configured for AUTOSAR code generation if both of the the following conditions exist: The model uses a Simulink.Signal  data object. The Simulink.Signal  object is configured with a custom storage class that is a reference to the PerInstanceMemory  custom storage class.

Summary: Error message This function is not C stub when using options -D POLYSPACE_NO_STANDARD_STUBS and -no-extern-C
Description: Consider, for example, the following C++ code. typedef unsigned int size_t; extern "C" { void *malloc(size_t ); }; int main(void) { volatile char buffer[4]; return *buffer; }  If you run a verification using the options -D POLYSPACE_NO_STANDARD_STUBS  and -no-extern-C , the verification fails. You see the following message in the log file: **** GNU C++ source normalization - 2 (P_INIT) ---------------------------------------------------------------------- @FFT: This function is not C stub : malloc__FUi |An internal consistency problem has been detected. |In order to avoid generating incomplete verification information, |the computation has been halted.    The function highlighted depends on the source code. ?
Workaround: To workaround the issue, rerun the verification without using the option -no-extern-C.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink generates incorrect multi-tasking code if a slow-rate input of a multi-rate For Each Subsystem comes from a code reuse subsystem output
Description: Simulink generates incorrect multi-tasking code if a model meets all  of the following conditions: The model has a For Each Subsystem containing multiple sample times. Any input signal of this For Each Subsystem is fed directly from an output port of an Atomic Subsystem at a sample rate slower than the fastest sample rate in the For Each Subsystem. That Atomic Subsystem has its Function packaging   parameter set to Reusable function . The Configuration Parameters -> Tasking mode for periodic sample times   parameter is set to Auto  or MultiTasking .
Workaround: Put a Signal Conversion block between the code reuse subsystem output and the For Each subsystem input which satisifies the conditions described above. Uncheck the Signal Conversion block's parameter Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization.Tip: On the block's Properties dialog box, set the Tag and Annotation properties to something unique, such as g781833Workaround, so that it can be identified and removed when this bug is addressed.

Summary: Bus Selector block dialog box fails to load when the input bus signal has circular definition
Description: If a bus signal has circular definition via feedback loops, the Bus Selector block fails to load the dialog box. An incorrect error dialog box appears. Attempting to open the dialog crashes MATLAB. There is a similar issue with the same root cause that can make opening the Bus Selector dialog very slow. For more information, see EBR 874385 .
Workaround: Insert a Signal Specification block in the signal that connects to the problematic Bus Selector block. For the Signal Specification block, specify a bus object as the data type.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Enabled subsystems connected to Matrix Concatenate block may produce incorrect code
Description: The model output may be incorrect when the following conditions are present: The output of a conditionally executed subsystem must be connected to the 2nd or higher input of a vector or matrix concat block The output port block should have the check box "Reset when disabled" selected The output port block is connected to a model reference, reusable atomic subsystem, s-function or a block with a matching TLC file in the rtw/c/tlc/blocks folder  The output is incorrect because it is not reset correctly in the Matrix Concatenate block output. It writes the wrong elements to the Matrix Concatenate block output. ?
Workaround: Force the output of the subsystem to be separate from the Matrix Concatenate output. For example, place a test point on the output of the subsystem.Replace the referenced model with a subsystem.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation with data store mappings
Description: When a Stateflow atomic subchart is reused multiple times with different data store mappings, then some of the reused instances might access the wrong data stores. In such cases, the generated code will not match the simulation results.
Workaround: Set the "Code generation function packaging" of the offending atomic subchart to "Inline".

Summary: xPC Target does not support the BOOL data type for EtherCAT devices.
Description: If an EtherCAT model uses the BOOL data type for a variable, an xPC Target application containing EtherCAT blocks can produce an initialization error. The first release of xPC Taraget EtherCAT support does not support the BOOL data type.

Summary: Why can't I use Intel Visual C++ Composer XE 2011 compiler with Microsoft Software Development Kit 7.1 linker for MEX-files in R2011b?
Description: MATLAB R2011b supports Intel Visual C++ Composer XE 2011 compiler with Microsoft Software Development Kit 7.1 linker on Windows 32-bit and 64-bit platforms. However, the compiler option is not displayed in mex -setup .
Workaround: On Windows 64-bit platforms:Open attached .ZIP file.Copy the following files to the MATLABROOT\bin\win64\mexopts folder:intelc12mssdk71opts.batintelc12mssdk71opts.stpintelc12mssdk71engmatopts.batOn Windows 32-bit platforms:Open attached .ZIP file.Copy the following files to the MATLABROOT\bin\win32\mexopts folder:intelc12mssdk71opts.bat.32intelc12mssdk71opts.stp.32intelc12mssdk71engmatopts.bat.32Remove the .32 extension from the file names, which become:intelc12mssdk71opts.batintelc12mssdk71opts.stpintelc12mssdk71engmatopts.bat.

Summary: Incorrect code for static signals with more than INT_MAX elements
Description: Operations on signals larger than INT_MAX might cause incorrect indexing code due to iteration variables of type signed int . The indexing might be incorrect because of overflows in the loop's increment operation. For example, adding two such arrays or adding a scalar signal to one such array might generate the incorrect code.
Workaround: Avoid using array signals with a size larger than INT_MAX.For release R2012a and beyond an error message is thrown. You can use the following feature setting to allow code generation to proceed. Manually examine the generated code for all uses of the wide signal described above.slfeature('AllowUnsafeOversizedMatrices', 1)To re-enable the error condition:slfeature('AllowUnsafeOversizedMatrices', 0)?

Summary: Error during code generation when using custom code in Stateflow charts
Description: Code generation for Stateflow charts using custom code may fail with the following assertion: CGIR assertion 'cg_type_equals_type(falseExpr->type(), aConditionalExpr->type())' failed in '.\dom_core\utility\cg_sanity_checker.cpp:1797'[  0] 0x505396b2          C:\MATLAB\R2011b\bin\win32\cgir_xform.dll+01414834 ( CG::SanityChecker::sanityCheckConditionalExpr+000658 )  This assertion is triggered when a custom code variable is used in the Stateflow action language inside the min  function.
Workaround: To work around this issue, you can disable the checks by executing the following command in MATLAB:slfeature('CGIRSanityChecker', 0)

Summary: Why does netcdf error out in R2011b when reading an empty variable?
Description: If a netcdf  variable is empty, netcdf.getVar  errors out in R2011b when earlier releases of MATLAB did not. This is due to a change in the netCDF library version from 4.0.1 in R2011a to 4.1.2 in R2011b. A netCDF variable can be empty if it has an unlimited dimension that has a length of zero. When the workaround below is applied, MATLAB will return [] when reading empty variables.

Summary: Bus type in MATLAB Function block may crash Simulink
Description: Simulink may crash during a model update diagram, if a top model contains a Model Reference block, which includes a MATLAB Function block, and the MATLAB Function block performs one of the following operations with a bus signal: Creates an array of buses inside the MATLAB Function block, and uses it entirely within the MATLAB Function block Creates a struct  containing a bus and uses it entirely within the MATLAB Function block Pass a bus to a non-inlined MATLAB function Return a bus as the output of a non-inlined MATLAB function
Workaround: If you pass or return a bus from a non-inlined function, specify the function for inlining using the command:coder.inline('always')If you construct an array, or struct, of buses, pass the array or struct to the Simulink model, or construct it in Simulink and pass it to the MATLAB Function block.

Summary: Subsystem name for library systems might cause TLC error
Description: A TLC error might be generated under all of the following conditions: The model has a multiple links to the same library system. The library system has Function packaging  set to Use subsystem name . The subsystem name is not a valid C identifier.
Workaround: Break all links between the model and the library using SET_PARAM.You can use the following example code, where mdl is the name of the model:open(mdl); blocks = find_system(mdl,'SearchDepth',50,'FollowLinks','On','Type','block'); for i = 1:length(blocks);  set_param(blocks{i}, 'LinkStatus', 'none'); end?

Summary: Documentation search results display a 'File Not Found' error
Description: When searching the documentation, clicking a search result may display a 'File Not Found' error message.
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your Polyspace installation. Summary: Lookup Table Dynamic block output might be 'uint8' when dialog setting is 'double'
Description: Dropping a Lookup Table Dynamic block from the Simulink library into a model gives uint8  output even though the dialog setting is double . Changing the Output data type  to another setting and back to double  causes block output to be double .
Workaround: Open the Lookup Table Dynamic block dialog box.  On the Signal Attributes tab, you can change Output data type to another setting and back to double. Then the block output is double.

Summary: The subs command errors when all inputs are symbolic non-scalar arrays.
Description: The subs  command may error if all the inputs are non-scalar symbolic arrays. For example:  >> syms x y a; >> f = [x; a]; >> subs(f, [x y], exp([x y]))Error using sym/cat>checkDimensions (line 76) CAT arguments dimensions are not consistent.
Workaround: Use cell arrays instead of symbolic arrays. For example: >> syms x y a; >> f = [x; a]; >> subs(f, {x y}, {exp(x) exp(y)})

Summary: SimRF equivalent baseband models may incorrectly set a parameter when reopening a model

Summary: For models with a root-level control port and Data Store Read or Write blocks, running the Model Advisor check, "Check for potential ordering issues involving data store access" causes Simulink to hang

Summary: sldvmergeharness output model has incorrect simulation stop time
Description: The output model generated by sldvmergeharness  has an incorrect value for simulation stop time that matches the value of the simulation stop time in the first input model. The correct simulation stop time for the sldvmergeharness  output model is the largest simulation stop time out of all the input model values. The simulation stop time can be viewed on the Configuration Parameters dialog box in the Solver > Simulation time  pane.

Summary: The documentation page for the function colorbar has an error in the syntax description.

Workaround: MATLAB? does not support this syntax. The function set may be used to set properties of a colorbar.

Summary: Loading data for root input ports in MATLAB timeseries format produces incorrect results if the timeseries IsTimeFirst property differs from the default value
Description: The MATLAB timeseries Data  property allows the time dimension to be first or last. For example, you can represent a 3x2 signal with 200 time points in either of these ways:  3x2x200 (IsTimeFirst = 0) 200x3x2 (IsTimeFirst = 1)  Simulink requires the time dimension to be as follows:  If the signal is a scalar or a 1-D vector, time must be the first dimension. If the signal has more than 1 dimension, time must be the last dimension.  Loading data for a timeseries that does not meet these conditions produces incorrect results.  When created or loaded from a file, a timeseries object that does not meet these requirements result in  a warning at the MATLAB prompt.  >> load sample_timeseries.mat Warning: In future releases the isTimeFirst property of this timeseries will be calculated based on the data and will differ from its current representation.
Workaround: To fix this problem, use the permute function to change the Data property of the timeseries such that: If the signal is a scalar or a 1-D vector, time is the first dimension.If the signal has more than 1 dimension, time is the last dimension.

Summary: Function isequal compares data object handles instead of property values
Description: When you use isequal  to compare two Simulink data objects, MATLAB does not compare the individual properties of the two data objects. Instead, it compares the two object handles. Consider the following example: a = Simulink.Parameter;b = Simulink.Parameter;isequal(a,b)ans = false Here, isequal  checks if the two variables (a  and b ) are pointing at the same object, and does not compare the properties of the two data objects. Note:  The incorrect behavior of isequal  is similar to the correct behavior of eq  (== ), which compares object handles.
Workaround: Use isContentEqual to compare individual properties of two Simulink data objects.a = Simulink.Parameter;b = Simulink.Parameter;isContentEqual(a,b)ans = true

Summary: Uninitialized iteration variable used in generated code for a For Each subsystem which contains a Switch or Multi-Port Switch block
Description: Code generated from Simulink Coder uses an uninitialized loop variable if a model meets all  of the following conditions: The Configuration Parameters > Optimization > Conditional input branch execution  parameter is checked. The model contains a For Each subsystem, which has a Switch or Multi-Port Switch block inside. A Merge block or Concatenate block ouside of this For Each subsystem is conditionally executed by the Switch or Multi-Port Switch block. To identify such blocks, display the sorted order for blocks in the model (turn on Format > Block Displays > Sorted Order ). For example, if the Concatenate block displays its sorted order as 1:0  and the Switch block displays its order as 2:2{1} , then the leading 1  in 1:0  means that the Concatenate block executes within a conditional execution context set up by one of the Switch block's inputs (indicated by the trailing 1  in 2:2{1} ). At least one source of this Merge or Concatenate block is not conditionally executed by the Switch or Multiport Switch block. You can identify such a source by using the sorted order numbering described above. The Concatenate block satisfies all  the following conditions The output signal is a fixed-size signal. Input signals are either all real or all complex. One  of the following conditions is true: The parameter Number of inputs  is set to 1 . The parameter Mode  is set to Vector  or the parameter Concatenate dimension  is set to the highest dimension of all input signals when the parameter Mode  is set to Multidimensional array .      To check whether this issue affects your model, run the DetectForBugReport773027.p  script. See the Workaround  section below for details. ?
Workaround: Download and unzip the attached Attachment_773027.zip script to a folder and add the folder to the MATLAB path using the addpath command. Run the script on your model, using the following syntax:ret = DetectForBugReport773027('modelname')For a model that is unaffected by this issue, the script returns an empty array. For an affected model, the script returns an array of structures with the following fields:Block: A conditionally executed Merge or Concatenate block that causes use of an uninitialized iteration variable.Inputs: Cell array of Merge or Concatenate block inputs whose actual sources are not conditionally executed.SourceBlocks: Cell array of source blocks that feed the Merge or Concatenate block, but are not conditionally executed.CEOwner: The Switch or Multi-Port Switch block that conditionally executes the Merge or Concatenate block.CEInput: The Switch or Multi-Port Switch block input that conditionally executes the Merge or Concatenate block.ForEachSubsystem: The For Each subsystem that contains the Switch or Multi-Port Switch block.Use one of the following two workarounds in your affected model:For the Merge or Concatenate blocks in Block, place a Signal Conversion block in front of input port marked in Inputs. In the Block Parameters dialog box for the Signal Conversion block, set the Output parameter to Contiguous copy, and check the Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization parameter.Uncheck the Configuration Parameters > Optimization > Conditional input branch execution parameter.

Workaround: Use the following installation instructions to install a fix for this issue. See Specifying fonts for PDF Reports below for instructions on how to specify fonts after you install the fix.Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start ˇ°WinZipˇ± in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting ˇ°Run as administratorˇ±.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Specifying Fonts for PDF ReportsThe following example shows how to specify Perpetua as the body font for a PDF report generated on a Windows 7 system.Check whether the directory, $tempdir/rptgenFonts, exists on your system, where $tempdir is the path returned by the MATLAB tempdir function.If the directory does not exist, create it.Copy the Perpetua font file from C:/Windows/Fonts to $tempdir/rptgenFonts.If a PDF stylesheet does not exist for your report, create one.Add a Body Font Family element to the stylesheet.Select Edit as XML on the element's dialog box.Replace serif with Perpetua as the Body Font Family element's value.Save the stylesheet.Specify the stylesheet as the stylesheet of your report.Run the report.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier report has invalid expected output values for blocks with multiple outports.

Summary: Model's data type override settings change boolean data type of Simulink.Parameter objects
Description: Data objects of boolean  data type should remain unaffected by a model's data type override settings. However, a model's data type override settings can erroneously alter the boolean  data type of Simulink.Parameter  objects. Consider the following example. A Simulink.Parameter  object myParam  of data type boolean  and value 1  is defined in a model whose data type override parameter is set to double . This setting erroneously changes the boolean  data type of myParam  to double . As a result, the value of myParam  is stored in buffer in a form where only the last bit contains the number 1  and all the preceding bits contain zeros. However, a block that references myParam  expects a boolean value. The block starts reading the buffer from the start and only reads the number of bits that correspond to a boolean value. The block does not reach the number 1  stored in the last bit and therefore, ascertains the value of myParam  to be 0 .

Summary: The express settings in the Visual Studio Plugin do not properly handle a class name with underscore in it
Description: For a project which defines a class as: Test_Example::Test_Example(){}  The plugin sets up the option '-class-analyzer custom=Test-Example ' in the project.The underscore becomes a hyphen and then the analysis fails with no eligible class found.

Summary: Build error for AUTOSAR multirunnable model if outport has enumerated data type and Underspecified initialization detection is set to Simplified
Description: Code generation fails for an AUTOSAR multirunnable model if both of the following conditions exist: The Simulink diagnostic Underspecified initialization detection  (UnderspecifiedInitializationDetection ) is set to Simplified . An outport within the runnable systems has an enumerated data type.  Under these conditions, the build process might display an error message similar to the following: When exporting runnables, the 'Initial output' value of block 'myModel/mySWC/Port1'' must be set to '0', '[]'. However, attempting to set the Initial output  value to an enumerated zero value or []  does not remedy the situation and can lead to further errors.

Summary: A vr.canvas object is visible only when you set figure units to pixels
Description: When you create a  vr.canvas  object in a figure that does not have its units set to pixels, the canvas is not visible in the figure. To display the canvas correctly, set the figure units to pixels.
Workaround: Set the figure units to pixels, or to use a unit other than pixels, install the attached patch:Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: A bug introduced in Release 2011b (7.13) slows down the performance of certain indexed assignments.
Description: The performance degradation occurs when you have an array that is large in size and there is a shared copy of this array. In the following code, the time to execute the last assignment 'big.data = 1 ' is significantly increased because of this bug.      shared = randn(10, 1e6);      big.data = shared;     big.data = 1; The presence of a shared variable triggers this problem. Without the shared variable, the performance of the last assignment is the same in R2011b and R2011a.
Workaround: To prevent this problem, start MATAB with the -norootrecord option.

Summary: Simulink.Bus, Simulink.Signal, and Simulink.Parameter objects lose their value when passed into a parfor loop
Description: When using Simulink.Bus , Simulink.Signal , or Simulink.Parameter  objects in a parfor  loop, their values are lost. A typical situation in which this may occur is when these objects and the models they are intended to be utilized with are inside a parfor  loop. In this situation, the objects are converted to doubles. ?

Summary: Indexed assignment of a cell array property to an object in an object array may lose data
Description: In rare circumstances, indexed assignment of a cell array property to an object in an object array loses data. For example:classdef bugclass  properties    p = {};  endendfunction res = bug  obj = bugclass;  x = 'xxx';  obj(1).p = {x};  class(obj(1));  res = obj.p;>> bugans =       Empty cell array: 0-by-1
Workaround: Disable cell array indexing in the JIT using this command:feature jitallow cell_array_indexing off

Summary: Using buildInstrumentedMex on MATLAB code that uses varargin may cause an assertion
Description: If you call buildInstrumentedMex to translate MATLAB code into a MEX function with instrumentation enabled, and the MATLAB code contains a function using varargin, MATLAB might assert with a message similar to      Assertion failed LocationLogging: input var has no location. at     line 840 of file "dom_eml/LocationLogging.cpp". followed by a stack trace.
Workaround: Modify the function to use an explicit list of arguments instead of varargin.

Workaround: Refer to the Workaround section of bug report 768047. The supplied patch may provide a workaround. See https://www.mathworks.com/support/bugreports/768047.?

Summary: Segmentation fault for a model containing a Stateflow chart with a loop
Description: A Stateflow chart might cause a segmentation fault during code generation when all of the following are true: On the Code Generation > Comments  pane, the Include comments parameter  is cleared; or the Stateflow chart is contained in a referenced model in Accelerator mode. The Stateflow chart implements a loop with a loop count less than the value of the Loop unrolling threshold  parameter on the Optimization > Signals and Parameter  pane. The loop body contains one and only one if-then-else  construct.  When the Loop unrolling threshold  value is greater than the loop count of a loop, the code generator implements the loop as sequential statements instead of a for  loop.
Workaround: There are possible workarounds:Set the Loop unrolling threshold to a number less than the loop count of the loop in the Stateflow chart.Turn on comments generation by selecting Include comments on the Code Generation > Comments pane. This workaround is not applicable for simulation targets.If the Stateflow chart is in a referenced model, set the Simulation Mode to Normal for the Model block referencing that model.

Summary: The logged datatype for states with an enum datatype may be incorrect for states in referenced models
Description: When a model logs states, the logged state may have an incorrect datatype if all  of these conditions exist: There is a state with an enum  datatype. The state is contained in a referenced model. The referenced model is directly or indirectly referenced from a Model block that has the Simulation mode  parameter set to Normal .  In this case, for the states with an enum  datatype, the variable that is created in the base workspace may have values with the wrong datatype.
Workaround: For all Model blocks that directly or indirectly reference a models that contain states with enum datatype, set the Simulation mode parameter to Accelerator.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier Built-In Block Replacements fail for Switch and Multiport Switch blocks with Integer rounding mode set to Simplest
Polyspace verification incorrectly reports a MISRA-C rule violation 11.4 on cast between a pointer to void and a pointer to an object
Description: On the following sample, Polyspace raises a MISRA-C warning on rule 11.4 and should not: typedef unsigned char uint8;typedef struct { void const * const field; } st_t;extern void func (st_t const * var); void func (st_t const * var) {  uint8 const * local = (uint8 const *)var->field; /* a MISRA-C 11.4 warning raises here and should not */ } According to ANSI C Standard, a void pointer is not a pointer to object.  ?

Summary: The function strlen/__ps_builtin_strlen may return an incorrect value
Description: The function strlen/__ps_builtin_strlen  may return an incorrect value. For example: int main() {  char * x = malloc(4); volatile char alea = 0 ; int n ;  assert(x != 0) ;  x[0] = alea ; x[1] = alea ; x[2] = alea ; x[3] = 0 ;  n = __ps_builtin_strlen(x) ; } In this example, the variable n  could be 0, 1, 2 or 3, since alea  could be 0, but the value returned by strlen  is 3.

Summary: Simulation And Code Generation Mismatch: Unexpected output from ERT S-function with Boolean type replacement of type > 8 bits
Description: ERT S-function generation cannot properly handle Boolean type replacement with a type that is not 8 bits (int8  or uint8 ). Simulating that S-function may generate unexpected output in a model simulation.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Boolean data type replacements may result in incorrect values
Description: You can specify data type replacements in the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Code Generation  > Data Type Replacement  pane.  Boolean  data type replacement specifications other than uint8  may result in coder functions allocating internal storage buffers of different sizes.  Code using different sized buffers for the same boolean  data may cause incorrect results. ?
Workaround: Specify boolean data type replacement types as data type uint8.

Summary: Value of the option -includes-to-ignore is corrupted on project load when ˇ°allˇ± is used
Description: In the Polyspace project manager, if you choose ˇ°all ˇ± for the option -includes-to-ignore , the value gets modified to ˇ°C:\all ˇ± when you reload the project.
Workaround: Go to "Polyspace inner settings" -> "Other options" and enter "-includes-to-ignore all" as a parameter.

Summary: COMMSCOPE.EYEDIAGRAM gives unnecessary warning: "Specified MaximumAmplitude level is changed to ... to obtain uniform amplitude grid."
Description: COMMSCOPE.EYEDIAGRAM checks if the MaximumAmplitude, MinimumAmplitude, and AmplitudeResolution properties result in a uniform grid. If not, it issues a warning and adjusts the values. In some cases, due to numerical accuracy issues, the function may issue an unnecassery warning; however, the function still producces the correct results.

Summary: Polyspace verification incorrectly reports a MISRA-C rule violation 12.10 on preprocessor directives
Description: On the following sample, Polyspace raises a MISRA-C violation on rule 12.10 and should not: #define DIRECTIVE(a, b) ((0 >= (a)) && (0 <= (b)))  #if !DIRECTIVE(0,0)  /* The rule should not be raised for preprocessor directives? */#define YES 1  #endifint foo(void) { #ifdef YES  return 1;  #elsereturn 0;#endif}

Summary: Simulation And Code Generation Mismatch: Wrong simulation results for multi-level model referencing in Normal mode
Description: Simulating the top model with multiple levels of model referencing produces incorrect results if all  the following conditions are met: In the top model, the Configuration Parameters > Optimization > Signals and Parameters > Inline parameters  parameter is set to Off . A Model block (B), whose parent is not  the top model, has a mask. The Model arguments  parameter of Model block B references a mask parameter, and that mask parameter references a model argument of B's parent model. All referenced models in the model referencing hiearchy from the top model to B run in Normal mode.
Workaround: Use one of these workarounds:Configure the Model block in the top model to run in Accelerator mode.In the top model, set the Configuration Parameters > Optimization > Signals and Parameters > Inline parameters parameter of the top model to On.

Summary: Calling svd with one output argument can fail to converge.
Description: On rare occasions, calling svd  with one output argument can fail to converge. Given a particular matrix A, this bug will cause the following warning for R2010a and R2010b: s = svd(A)Warning: SVD did not converge at index = 2.  The warning message for R2011a and later releases is slightly different: s = svd(A)Warning: SVD did not converge.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Potential incorrect generated code for an Invoke AUTOSAR Server Operation block inside a reusable subsystem
Description: The build process might generate incorrect code for an Invoke AUTOSAR Server Operation block if all of the following conditions are true: The Invoke AUTOSAR Server Operation block is located inside a reusable subsystem. One of the block inputs is an array (a signal with two or more dimensions). The array input signal is directly connected to the input of the parent reusable subsystem.  Under these conditions, code generation completes, but compiling the generated code throws a warning containing text similar to the following: warning: passing argument 1 of 'Rte_Call_mdl_port_store_array' from incompatible pointer type ./stub/Rte_mdl.h:34: note: expected 'real_T (*)[4]' but argument is of type 'const real_T **' /l  ?

Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <POLYSPACE> folder.Linux Systems: Type the following in a shell: cd <POLYSPACE>unzip <.zip file>Check that binaries have appropriate ˇ®read & executeˇŻ permissions.Delete the .zip file in the <POLYSPACE> folder.Reopen the Polyspace verification environment.

Summary: "Attempt to reference field of non-structure array" error appears when right-clicking Stateflow chart background
Description: If you do not have a Simulink Verification and Validation license and you attempt to right-click the background of a Stateflow chart to reach the context menu, this error message appears: "Attempt to reference field of non-structure array. " This error relates to the Requirements  item in the context menu, which offers only a subset of actions without a valid Simulink Verification and Validation license installed.
Workaround: When possible, instead of right-clicking the Stateflow chart background, right-click the corresponding Stateflow block in parent Simulink block diagram to reach the context menu.If that is not possible, use the attached patch..Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Restart MATLAB. Make sure that toolbox path caching is not enabled in the File > Preferences > General > Toolbox path caching pane.
