Summary: Simulink Design Verifier Report might have invalid hyperlinks to replaced blocks if multiple block replacement rules exist
Description: In the generated Simulink Design Verifier Report for your model, the Block Replacements Summary might contain invalid hyperlinks to the replaced blocks if you specified multiple block replacement rules.

Summary: The pole pair value of PM Synchronous Motor Drive (AC6) block in the Electric Drives library is not updated correctly inside the model
Description: If you change the value of the Pole pairs  parameter of the PM Synchronous Motor Drive (AC6) block, the Vector controller block (the VECT block under the mask of the AC6 block) is not being updated with the new value. A value of 4 is hardcoded in the model. This leads to incorrect simulation results.
Workaround: The pole pair value of the VECT block under the mask of the PM Synchronous Motor Drive (AC6) block can be fixed with the ac6_update.m function provided in attachment. Download and unzip the attacnment, then typehelp ac6_update for more information on how to use this function.

Summary: Delay block produces wrong answers in Accelerator mode simulation for state width larger than 65536 and ASIC/FPGA production hardware
Description: To reproduce this bug, the following conditions must be true: The production hardware is ASIC/FPGA. Simulation mode is Accelerator or Rapid Accelerator. The width of the input signal to the Delay block is less than 65536. The product of the input signal width and the maximum Delay length  is larger than 65536. That is, the width of state for the Delay block is larger than 65536.  This bug does not impact normal code generation.
Workaround: Users can split one Delay block into a series of Delay blocks to keep the state width for each block less than 65536.

Summary: When HDL code is generated for the Viterbi Decoder with RAM-based Traceback, the reported latency between the HDL and the block output is larger than the actual delay.
Description: When the RAM-based Traceback architecture is chosen for the Viterbi block, the extra latency present in the HDL code with respect to the original Viterbi block is reported in the html report and used in the validation model. This number is larger than the actual delay introduced in the HDL code.
Workaround: To obtain the correct latency, subtract (TBD + 1) from the reported value, where TBD is the Traceback Depth specified in the Viterbi block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: MATLAB Coder might generate incorrect code when the error function is called with an empty first input argument.
Description: MATLAB Coder might generate incorrect code when you call the error  function with an empty first  input argument, as in this example:  error(nargchk(low,high,nargin)); when low  ˇÜ nargin  ˇÜ high . The problem occurs because the compiler incorrectly infers that the error function will halt execution.
Workaround: No action is required if the first input to the error function is a constant, non-empty string. Otherwise, either check for an empty input before passing it to the error function, or install the attached update.To check for an empty input, add a conditional statement such as:if ~isempty(s)  error(s); endTo install the attached update:Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start ˇ°WinZipˇ± in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting ˇ°Run as administratorˇ±.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: vision.GeometricTransformer System object may output incorrect image size
Description: When you set the 'OutputImagePositionSource' to 'Property' and 'OutputImagePosition' to a non-square region, the vision.GeometricTransformer System object may output incorrect image size. The System object incorrectly interprets the format of the 'OutputImagePosition' property as [ X Y HEIGHT WIDTH]. The format of this property is [X Y WIDTH HEIGHT].
Workaround: To workaround this problem for R2011b version of MATLAB?, specify the format for 'OutputImagePosition' property as [X Y HEIGHT WIDTH].

Summary: Annotations to indicate known violations of MISRA-C rules for preprocessing directives do not appear in MISRA-C-report.xml
Description: Suppose you annotate your code to indicate known violations of MISRA-C rules for preprocessing directives, for example, rules 19.1, 19.4, and 19.6. If you then run a verification and generate the MISRA-C-report.xml , your annotations do not appear in the report.
Workaround: To workaround the issue, use a block format for your code annotations. For example:#include <stdint.h> /* MISRA C 19.1 */ int16 i; /* polyspace:begin<MISRA-C:19.1,19.4,19.6:[Not a defect]:[Justify with annotations]> A justification comment */ #include "myheader.h" /* MISRA C 19.4 */ #define TST (uint8)0x00u, (uint8)0x00u /* MISRA C 19.6 */ #undef TST /* polyspace:end<MISRA-C:19.1,19.4,19.6:[Not a defect]:[Justify with annotations]> */ ?

Summary: GPUArray overload of MTIMES can return an incorrect answer for large data sets when a row vector is multiplied by a matrix
Description: For GPUArrays, if A  is 1-by-M, and B  is M-by-N, then C = A*B  is computed incorrectly when M > Mlimit  or when N > Nlimit . The values of Mlimit  and Nlimit  depend on the underlying class and complexity of the output, C : If C  is real and single: Mlimit  = 2,097,120, Nlimit  = 1,048,560 If C  is complex, double, or both: Mlimit  = 1,048,560, Nlimit  = 524,280  Elements of the output below these limits are correct. Elements beyond these limits are set incorrectly.
Workaround: You can use the following workaround to compute the correct product of A and B:C = (B.' * A.').'

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier analysis generates error when Model blocks reference models with From/Goto blocks that have matching tags
Description: If you run Simulink Design Verifier analysis on a model that contains a Model block, the error message "Invalid Simulink object name " appears when: The parent model and the referenced model contain From/Goto blocks with matching tags. The referenced model contains From/Goto blocks with global tag visibility. The From/Goto blocks in the referenced model are inside of a Subsystem block whose name ends in the name of the referenced model.  The parent model is the top-level model. The referenced model is inside the Model block.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when running a SimEvents model that contains a link to an unsaved library
Description: If you run a SimEvents model when all of these conditions are true, MATLAB crashes: The SimEvents model is either newly created in R2011b or later, or has been updated to the the latest version of SimEvents using the seupdate  utility. The model contains a library-linked subystem block that contains SimEvents blocks. The linked library is open and has unsaved changes.  The crash does not occur for models created in releases prior to R2011b, even if they are run in R2011b.
Workaround: To work around this issue, save all changes to libraries containing SimEvents blocks before running your model.

Summary: Applications using fdesign or fdesign-based objects may produce runtime errors
Description: When you compile an application containing fdesign  code or a filter object designed with fdesign , the application may error at run time. This error also affects applications designed with interactive tools such as filterbuilder and FDATool. Examples of the error messages you may see at run time are: Undefined function or variable 'fmethod.sqrtrcoswin'. Error in ==> fdesign.abstracttype.superdesign at 68 Error in ==> fdesign.abstractpulseshape.design at 9 Error in ==> fdesign.pulseshaping.design at 11 You can observe this problem in versions R2010b and earlier. The affected filter responses are: fdesign.audioweighting fdesign.polysrc fdesign.lowpass (for Kaiser window designs) fdesign.arbgrpdelay (for IIR lp-norm designs) fdesign.arbmagnphase (for FIR least squares, IIR least squares, frequency sampling, and equiripple designs) fdesign.octave fdesign.pulseshaping
Workaround: Add the following directories manually to the shared resources of the project:matlabroot\toolbox\signal\signal\@fdesignmatlabroot\toolbox\signal\sigtools\@fmethodmatlabroot\toolbox\dsp\filterdesign\@fdfmethodHere matlabroot is the string returned by the matlabroot command.

Summary: COMMSCOPE.EYEDIAGRAM produces wrong results if the name-value pair property inputs are not in a certain order
Description: COMMSCOPE.EYEDIAGRAM accepts name-value pairs as its input. For example, commscope.eyediagram('SamplingFrequency', 100, 'PlotTimeOffset', 0.01) sets the SamplingFrequency property to 100 and PlotTimeOfset property to 0.01. If you list PlotTimeOffset property before the SamplingFrequency property, the resulting plot does not represent the correct time offset.

Summary: Missing example template for Simulink Project if you previously tried R2011b prerelease
Description: If you tried Simulink Projects in the R2011b pre-release, then the example template "Project Environment" is not available when you create a new Simulink Project.
Workaround: You can fix the problem by running the following MATLAB code:>> src = fullfile(matlabroot, 'toolbox','simulink','simulink','slproject','templates','project_startup_shutdown.zip');>> dst = fullfile(prefdir, 'sl_project_templates','project_startup_shutdown.zip');>> copyfile(src, dst, 'f') If you previously created any templates, they will not be overwritten.Next, you need to validate the template and make it visible. Open the Simulink Project user interface and select the menu item Simulink Project > Templates > Template Manager. Right-click the template "project_startup_shutdown.zip" and select "Validate". Right-click the template "project_startup_shutdown.zip" and select "Set Visible".Now you can create a new project using the example template. Open the Simulink Project user interface and select the menu item Simulink Project > New. Click "Browse" to select a template. You can now select the "Project Environment" template.

Summary: Bus Selector and Bus Assignment Blocks fail to select signals with '/' character.
Description: Bus Selector and Bus Assignment blocks with a selected signal whose name contains a forward slash (/ ) character replace that character with a period (. ). This name change causes the model to fail to update or simulate.
Workaround: Install the attached patch file.Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start ˇ°WinZipˇ± in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting ˇ°Run as administratorˇ±.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Change logSeptember 20, 2011: attachment_763299_2011-09-20.zip: updated fix to address '/' being at the beginning of the bus element name. September 19, 2011: attachment_763299_2011-09-19.zip: initial version, works on R2011b only.

Summary: Fatal error: "call to an unknown procedure which was not properly stubbed: pow" when using the flag POLYSPACE_NO_STANDARD_STUBS
Description: When the function pow() exists in an application and you set the compilation flag POLYSPACE_NO_STANDARD_STUBS, verification can produce the following error: **** Visual C++ 7.1 to intermediate language translation - 17 (P_VT) took 0real, 0u + 0s (0gc)**** Visual C++ 7.1 to intermediate language translation - 18 (P_PT) ---------------------------------------------------------------------- @FFT: * fatal error, file: source_file.c, 4907:43 : | call to an unknown procedure which was not properly stubbed: pow |An internal consistency problem has been detected.
Workaround: Re-launch the verification without the flag POLYSPACE_NO_STANDARD_STUBS.If you have link issues with standard functions, use the option -no-extern-C, and add the compilation flag __polyspace_no_fopen.

Summary: The interp2, interp3, and interpn functions might return incorrect results when you use the 'nearest' method and interpolate outside the grid near the boundary
Description: This behavior only arises when you interpolate using the 'nearest ' method and choose query points outside the grid, but within a distance of 0.5 from the boundary. The following example demonstrates the problem. [X, Y] = ndgrid(1:5);V = X.^2 + Y.^2;vq = interpn(X,Y,V,4,5.4, 'nearest') vq =    41 The query point (4, 5.4) is outside the domain of the grid, so the result should be NaN  instead of 41 .

Summary: Opening a model containing a block from custom Simscape library can result in a MATLAB crash
Description: Opening a model containing a block from custom Simscape library whose port interface in the custom library has been changed can result in a MATLAB crash.
Workaround: The model can be fixed with the workaround_762729.m function provided in the attachment. 1. Make a backup copy of the model before applying this workaround.2. Download and unzip the attachment, then typehelp workaround_762729for more information on how to use this function.

Summary: Taking the min or max of a column of GPU data that is all NaN, incorrectly gives Inf instead of NaN.
Description: When using min  or max  to find the minimum or maximum value of a column of GPU data, columns that are all NaN  incorrectly produce Inf  (for min ) or -Inf  (for max ) results instead of NaN . For example, this code displays the incorrect result:  data = gpuArray([1 nan 2; 3 nan nan]);min(data)ans =      1   Inf    2 The correct result should be:  ans =      1   NaN    2 This does not affect calculations along rows, or calculations where at least one value is not NaN .
Workaround: Use isfinite instead of isnan to check for finite values in the output of min or max on the GPU.

Summary: MATLAB? crashes when passing a non double array to plot, loglog, semilogx or semilogy.
Description: This problem is encountered when using a two variable syntax for the plot functions e.g., plot(x,y) and it satisfies both these conditions 1. If 'y' is not of type 'double' 2. If the shape of 'y' requires a transpose to match 'x' dimensions, MATLAB crashes when you try to issue e.g.,  x=1:4; y=single([1:4;2:5]); loglog(x,y)  This is because, when 'y' is a double array, MATLAB? will transpose 'y' to match with dimensions of 'x' and produce number of lineseries objects equal to the number of rows in 'y'.
Workaround: Since MATLAB? does not transpose 'y' matrix automatically when it is not double, manually transpose 'y' and plot it as,x=1:4; % x is a 1-by-4 vector of doubley=single([1:4;2:5]);% y is a 2-by-4 array of singley = y'; % Transposes y to match number of rows of y to columns of x.loglog(x,y)

Summary: maxflat errors when designing a 1st order FIR filter or a 2nd order symmetric FIR filter
Description: The following commands error with the message:  ??? Attempted to access table(:,4); index out of bounds because size(table)=[0,0].  maxflat(1,0,0.5)  or maxflat(2,'sym',0.5)  This bug also appears when the user specifies the 'maxflat' option through fdesign. d = fdesign.lowpass( 'N,F3dB', 2, 20, 1125 );  Hd = design(d,'maxflat');

Summary: The history and timeseries methods of the blp class return incorrect date and time stamps
Description: The history  and timeseries  methods of the blp  class can return incorrect date and time stamps. Occurrence of this behavior depends on the time zone from which you make a data request. history  can return dates that are one day early. timeseries  can return time stamps that are incorrect by fractions of seconds. For example, the time 10:30:00 can appear as 10:29:59.999.
Workaround: Rename the files:$MATLAB/toolbox/datafeed/datafeed/@blp/blp.p$MATLAB/toolbox/datafeed/datafeed/@blp/history.m$MATLAB/toolbox/datafeed/datafeed/@blp/timeseries.m$MATLAB/java/jar/toolbox/datafeed.jarReplace the above files with the files in the attachment. In MATLAB, type:rehash toolboxcache?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: MATLAB Coder might generate incorrect code for complex-valued functions with side effects
Description: MATLAB Coder might generate incorrect code for functions that meet both the following conditions: Are used as part of a complex-valued computation Produce a side effect when they are called (for example, they write to a persistent or global variable, call an external C function, etc.)  It is possible that the side effect will be duplicated in the generated code.     function x = count() %#codegen     persistent z;     if (isempty(z))       z = 0;     end     z = z + 1;     x = z;   end    c = complex(a,b);   y = c .* count(); In this example, the persistent variable might be incorrectly incremented twice in the generated code.
Workaround: As a workaround, assign the results of the function to a temporary variable.    c = complex(a,b);   tmp = count();   y = c .* tmp;

Summary: Simulink might fail to simulate a referenced model block in accelerated mode
Description: Simulink might fail to simulate a referenced model block or fail to generate code for a model using the model reference simulation target. This can happen if the referenced model contains a MATLAB block and one of its inputs is a Simulink bus signal. Simulink displays the error message Code generation assertion 'numUnpaddedFields < numPaddedFields'  followed by a stack trace.
Workaround: There are two possible workarounds:Change the reference model block parameter Simulation mode from Acclerated to Normal.Avoid bus signals with unaligned structure elements. To do this, manually insert unused structure elements to enforce an 8-byte alignment, which is required by Simulink. For example, manually align the following strucuture:>> struct('data', single(2), 'status', false(2,1))ans =data: 2 status: [2x1 logical]by inserting an unused logical field with 6 elements>> struct('data', single(2), 'status', false(2,1), 'unused', false(6,1))ans =data: 2 status: [2x1 logical] unused: [6x1 logical]?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: MATLAB Coder might generate incorrect code for real-valued functions that take complex arguments
Description: MATLAB Coder might generate incorrect code for a real-valued function that takes a complex argument if both the following conditions are true: The function is used as part of an expression that is assigned to a complex variable This complex variable is passed as an argument to the function  For example:    function magSq = magnitude_squared(x) %#codegen      magSq = real(x) .* real(x) + imag(x) .* imag(x);   end    c = (1 + 2i) .* magnitude_squared(c);
Workaround: As a workaround, assign the result of the function call to a temporary variable, and use that variable in place of the function call:   tmp = magnitude_squared(c);   c = (1 + 2i) .* tmp;

Summary: Initializing nonvirtual buses using a scalar enumerated value that corresponds to numeric zero results in an error when generating code
Description: Generating code when both  of these conditions exist throws an error:  A nonvirtual bus composed of elements of the same enumerated type is passed through a Memory or Unit Delay block. Such a Memory or Unit Delay block specifies has its Initial condition  parameter set to the numeric zero value of this enumerated type.  The error is of the form:  'MyModel/UnitDelayBlock' cannot convert parameter X0 from data type 'MyEnumeratedType' to data type 'BusObject' because conversion of parameters with enumerated data types is not supported
Workaround: For the Memory or Unit Delay block, set the Initial condition parameter to use a MATLAB structure (instead of a scalar value). For information about how to create a MATLAB structure from a bus object, see the Simulink.Bus.createMATLABStruct documentation.

Summary: Code Generation might fail for models with Verification Subsystem blocks containing blocks that depend on absolute time
Description: "Build Model" fails in Code Generation of a Simulink model if all of the following conditions exist: the model contains Verification Subsystem blocks (from Simulink Design Verifier). any of those Verification Subsystem blocks contain blocks that depend on absolute time. the option to support absolute time in code generation is not selected.  The MATLAB command window displays a printout of the following form, reporting code generation build failure: ### Starting build procedure for model: mymodel### Generating code into build folder: C:\Documents\MATLAB\mymodel_ert_rtw### Build procedure for model: 'mymodel' aborted due to an error. The error message looks like the following: Block 'mymodel/Verification Subsystem/To File' uses absolute time, which is not supported with the current configuration. Consider selecting the support 'absolute time' option on the Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Interface page
Workaround: If a Verification Subsystem block contains blocks that depend on absolute time, and you select an ERT-based target for code generation, open the Configuration Parameters dialog box and on the Code Generation > Interface > Software environment pane, select absolute time. Do not select continuous time.

Summary: "Error using rethrow" appears when right-clicking masked subsystem in Simulink function in Stateflow chart
Description: If model coverage is turned on and you right-click a masked subsystem in a Simulink function in a Stateflow chart, no context menu appears and the MATLAB Command Window displays the following error message: Error using rethrow Input must be a structure. Error in SlCov.FilterEditor/getProperties (line 87)  rethrow MEx; Error in SlVnv.FilterEditor/isFilteredIntern (line 158)  props = this.getProperties(ssid); Error in SlVnv.FilterEditor/isFilteredByAncestors (line 9)  [res prop] = this.isFilteredIntern(allAncs{idx}); Error in SlCov.CovMenus.contextMenu>addCoverageFilterMenus (line 143)  [res pprop]= filter.isFilteredByAncestors(ssid); Error in SlCov.CovMenus.contextMenu (line 28)  actionSchemas = addCoverageFilterMenus(sel, topModelName); You can edit your coverage settings for the entire model from the menu bar under Tools > Coverage Settings . You can edit coverage settings for a specific object within the model from the Coverage  item in the context menu that appears when you right-click an object. ?

Summary: Initialization code for signal with FileScope (Custom) storage class in reusable subsystem causes compilation error
Description: An error occurs when compiling generated code where a signal's initialization code is placed inside the model source file, instead of the subsystem source file. This compilation error occurs for a model under all of the following conditions: An atomic subsystem is generated to its own file. The atomic subsystem contains a signal, which has the storage class FileScope (Custom) . The subsystem parameter Function packaging  is set to Reusable function .
Workaround: On the Code Generation pane of the subsystem parameter dialog box, specify Function packaging as Function. You can make this change without losing any reuse opportunities, because a model cannot contain more than one subsystem which contains a signal specified with the FileScope(Custom) storage class.

Summary: Linearization errors when model has Discrete Filter block with nonscalar input
Description: Linearizing a model generates an error if the model has a Discrete Filter block whose input signal is not scalar. Linearizing the model using the linearize  command might result in the following error: Error using LinearizationObjects.linearizationutil/utOrderNameStates (line 27) In the "linearize(modelname,'StateOrder',StateOrder)" StateOrder must be a cell array of valid Simulink blocks or state names in the model linearize_filter. The block or state name linearize_filter/Filter 2nd Order is not valid.  Linearizing the model using Linear Analysis Tool in R2011b or using Control and Estimation Tools Manager in previous releases results in the following error: The state order specified contains states that are no longer in the Simulink model. Remove these states from the Linearization State Ordering tab in the Options Dialog.
Workaround: To work around this error, revise your model to ensure that the Discrete Filter block only receives scalar inputs. To do this:Extract the vector signal input into scalar signals using the Demux block.Use a separate Discrete Filter block for each of the resulting scalar signals.Recombine the outputs of the Discrete Filter blocks into a vector signal using a Mux block.

Summary: matlabsub returns incorrect results
Description: Multiple calls to the matlabsub  function in an Excel spreadsheet return the same value when the spreadsheet is recalculated. For example, if the MATLAB rand  function is used in three different matlabsub  calls in a spreadsheet, all three calls return the same value when they should return different values.

Summary: C2803x CAN Calibration Protocol (CPP) block fails to generate code.?
Description: Code generation fails if you use the C2803x CCP block in your model.

Summary: URL hyperlinks in PDF reports do not work
Description: In PDF reports, URL links generated by the Link component do not work.
Workaround: Install the attached file.Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. Summary: Polyspace verification reports an incorrect message associated with UNR check on conditional instructions of a loop
Description: On the following code, Polyspace for C displays an incorrect UNR message on a while()  loop. The message says "unreachable code" even if code is reacheable: int main(void) {   int a = 0;   int b = 0;   while (!a || !b) { /* Incorrect UNR check on !b */   if (a) b = 1;   a = 1;   } } At the first iteration of while() loop, b is not read since !a == 1. Then a is set to 1. At the second iteration, !a == 0, and so !b is read and reachable.

Summary: CFTOOL plotting error: "Complex inputs are not supported"
Description: When using custom equations in CFTOOL, you can see an error message like the following if your curve or surface fitting model makes complex (non-zero imaginary part) predictions:  This is not a valid NumericMatrix value. Complex inputs are not supported. CFTOOL does not plot a fit and you see an error at the command line, because CFTOOL cannot plot complex (non-zero imaginary part) predictions.
Workaround: To avoid this error, modify your custom equation so that only real-valued outputs are predicted, or replace complex elements with NaN.You can use the attached function, complexToNaN, as follows: if your custom equation is f(x), replace it with complexToNaN( f(x) ).For example: if you are using the following custom equation to fit a curve:(E_max*x^n1)/(EC_50^n1 + x^n1) - (I_max*x^n2)/(IC_50^n2 + x^n2)With non-integer values for n1 or n2, this will make complex (non-zero imaginary part) predictions when x is negative. In this model, x represents concentrations of a drug, so negative values of x are not meaningful. Hence you can replace these complex valued predictions by NaN. To do this, replace the above custom equation withcomplexToNaN( (E_max*x^n1)/(EC_50^n1 + x^n1) - (I_max*x^n2)/(IC_50^n2 + x^n2) ).You need to download the attached MATLAB function, complexToNaN, and put it on your MATLAB path.

Summary: Simulation And Code Generation Mismatch: Generated code for Iterator Subsystem driving root-level Outport block in simplified initialization mode may contain uninitialized local variables
Description: Simulink Coder may generate code with uninitialized local variables when all  of the following conditions exist: An Iterator Subsystem drives a root-level Outport block, directly or indirectly through a Bus Creator, Bus Selector, or Signal Conversion block. The Iterator Subsystem is configured to run at least once at every time step. For example, a For Iterator Subsystem with the iteration limit of at least 1. The model uses simplified initialization mode (Configuration Parameters > Diagnostics > Data Validity > Underspecified initialization detection  parameter is set to Simplified ).  When generating code such a model, the compiler may throw a warning similar to the one below. t:\1-fd3g47\slprj\ert\ref10b\ref10b.c(35) : warning C4700: uninitialized local variable 'rtb_sig2' used
Workaround: There are two possible workarounds:Insert a block between the Iterator Subsystem and the root-level Outport block. For the inserted block, use a block that produces an identity transformation. For example, insert a Reshape block configured to output the same dimensions as the input, or insert a Bias block with zero bias.Turn off the simplified initialization mode: set Configuration Parameters > Diagnostics > Data Validity > Underspecified initialization detection to Classic.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Signal connected to Unit Delay or Memory block on both sides of a subsystem boundary
Description: The generated code is incorrect when a model contains all of the following: A signal, with two or more destination blocks, crosses a subsystem boundary. The signal is implemented as a single global buffer through specification. The signal is an input to either a Unit Delay or a Memory block on both sides of the subsystem boundary. The Unit Delay or Memory blocks have an initial condition of zero.  For an example, see the attached model mIncorrectStateReuse.mdl . Specifically, please notice that the state of Unit Delay1  in Subsystem1  is optimized away incorrectly.
Workaround: Turn off the code generation optimization that causes the incorrect code by doing one of the following:To turn off the optimization globally, download the attached MATLAB script WorkaroundForBugReport760112.p. To enable the workaround, execute the script WorkaroundForBugReport760112(true); to disable the workaround, execute the script WorkaroundForBugReport760112(false).To turn off the optimization locally, insert a Signal Conversion block at the input of the Unit Delay or Memory block that triggers the bug.Detect the Unit Delay or Memory block usage that could trigger the bug by doing the following:Download the attached MATLAB script DetectForBugReport760112.p;Run the script on the model DetectForBugReport760112(model);Verify that the code generated for the detected block is correct.

Summary: S-function target limitation: aligning structure padding for an S-function with bus input or output signals
Description: Real-Time Workshop and Simulink Coder documentation should note the following as an S-function target software limitation: If an S-function generated using the S-function target has bus input or output signals, the generated bus data structures might include padding to align fields of the bus elements with the Simulink representation used during simulation. However, if you insert the S-function in a model and generate code using a model target such as grt.tlc , the bus structure alignment generated for the model build might be incompatible with the padding generated for the S-function and might affect the numerical results of code execution.
Workaround: To make the structure alignment consistent between model simulation and execution of the model code, for each Simulink.Bus object, you can modify the HeaderFile property to remove the unpadded bus structure header file. This will cause the bus typedefs generated for the S-function to be reused in the model code.

Summary: Up Arrow and Down Arrow keys do not work within the Current Folder browser
Description: Up Arrow  and Down Arrow  keys do not change the selection in the Current Folder browser. This problem includes Shift+Up Arrow  and Shift+Down Arrow  for file selection.
Workaround: Mouse selection, the Left Arrow and Right Arrow keys, and the Page Up and Page Down keys function correctly.

Summary: Segmented Pipeline and Segmented Pipe LP blocks do not respect the setting of the Pipe wall type parameter
Description: The Segmented Pipeline and Segmented Pipe LP blocks contain a parameter named Pipe wall type . This setting determines if the walls of the pipe are rigid or flexible. When simulating or generating code, the pipe walls are considered rigid regardless of this setting.
Workaround: To model a Segmented Pipeline with flexible pipe walls, create an equivalent lumped parameter pipeline model using alternating Hydraulic Pipeline and Fluid Inertia blocks connected in series as shown on the block reference page.Similarly, to model a Segmented Pipe LP with flexible pipe walls, create an equivalent lumped parameter pipeline model using alternating Hydraulic Pipe LP and Fluid Inertia blocks connected in series as shown on the block reference page.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: In rare situations Stateflow fails to generate early return logic
Description: Stateflow fails to generate early return logic under rare conditions when undirected local events are broadcast in the entry or exit actions of a state. In case of entry actions the following criterion have to be met:Undirected local event broadcast in entry action. An additional implicit entry event must be defined for the same state.    In case of exit actions, the criterion are as follows:Undirected local event broadcast in exit action. Execution of this local event broadcast must cause the state A to be exited.  For an example of how incorrect early return can affect your simulation, see the attached model. In the model, if the early return logic is inserted correctly, broadcast of event E2 should lead to state C being active and the value of out  should 1.  Note that, states in a Stateflow chart which do not meet this criterion will continue to generate correct early return logic.

Summary: Folder or file names with character @ are not supported by SVN integration of Simulink Project
Description: A folder or file in an SVN sandbox cannot be added to a Simulink Project if the folder or file name contains the character @.
Workaround: Turn off SVN integration:From the Simulink Project tree, select the Source Control node.Under Available Source Control Integrations, select No Source Control Integration. Click Reload.

Summary: int and simplify can be slow when finding high-degree algebraic dependency
Description: Executing the following commands takes a very long time: syms Tf = 1/(T^2*cosh((74441/100)*1/T)^2) + 1/(T^2*sinh((8456/5)*1/T)^2);g = 1/(T^2*cosh((74441/100)*T)^2) + 1/(T^2*sinh((8456/5)*T)^2);simplify(f), simplify(g)int(f, T), int(g, T)
Workaround: In simple cases like this one, split the input by linearity to avoid treating multiple trigonometric or hyperbolic functions with related arguments at the same time:syms Tf1 = 1/(T^2*cosh((74441/100)*1/T)^2);f2 = 1/(T^2*sinh((8456/5)*1/T)^2);g1 = 1/(T^2*cosh((74441/100)*T)^2);g2 = 1/(T^2*sinh((8456/5)*T)^2);simplify(f1) + simplify(f2), simplify(g1) + simplify(g2)int(f1, T) + int(f2, T), int(g1, T) + int(g2, T)

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Constant block of bus data type and value 0 may cause incorrect simulation results or MATLAB crash
Description: Simulating or generating code for a model that satisfies the following conditions may cause incorrect simulation results, MATLAB crash, or incorrect code generation. The model contains a Constant block of bus data type. The Constant value  parameter of this block is 0 .
Workaround: If you want to specify the Constant value parameter of a Constant block to be the ground (zero representation) value of the block's output bus data type, use a MATLAB structure instead of specifying the scalar value 0.Use the function Simulink.Bus.createMATLABStruct to create a MATLAB structure from a bus object.

Summary: renderwebfigure terminates with errors when running in deployed mode
Description: When a function calling renderwebfigure  is deployed, it throws an error similar to the following: Unable to initialize com.mathworks.webintegration.vrd.LicenseActionsFactory Error using internal.matlab.Message/getString Unable to load a message catalog 'Compiler:renderwebfigure'. Please check the file location and format. Error in renderwebfigure>cropFigureAndReturnCData (line 64) Error in renderwebfigure (line 31)  You may also see the message: Issue displaying image. Please refresh.   ?
Workaround: To workaround this issue, extract the ZIP archive at the location MCR_ROOT\resources where MCR_ROOT is the root directory where the MATLAB Compiler Runtime (MCR) is installed.

Summary: Connecting to a remote OPC HDA server can generate the error: "Failed to connect HDA client object to server"
Description: Attempting to connect to an OPC HDA server that is on a remote machine might generate this error: ??? Error using ==> Client>Client.connect at 314Failed to connect HDA client object to server. Connecting to a Data Access (DA) server on the same machine is successful.
Workaround: To work around this problem, use these steps to download an attached .zip file and install its contents:Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation.Start MATLAB and locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the appropriate attached .zip file from this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder. Be sure to download the correct file for your MATLAB version and operating system.View the contents of the .zip file and make a backup copy of each of the original corresponding files in your <MATLAB> folder.Extract the content from the .zip file into your <MATLAB> folder.Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Diesel Engine In-Line Injection System demo does not work
Description: When you try runnig the Diesel Engine In-Line Injection System demo, it misses the cylinder fire points and the simulation is very slow.
Workaround: The get the demo working properly, please change spring rate of the TS1 spring in all four Injector models to 19.2e4 N/m.

Summary: Number of successfully analyzed Simulink Design Verifier Test Generation objectives might decrease compared to number from previous release
Description: In Test Generation analysis, the number of objectives satisfied or proven false by Simulink Design Verifier might decrease compared to the number successfully analyzed in a previous release. This occurs if your model contains counters or timers, since these tend to involve time delays. If other states are changing while counters are updating, Simulink Design Verifier Test Generation analysis might report a much lower number of objectives satisfied or proven false than it did in a previous release.
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Instructions for Applying WorkaroundAfter installing the .zip file, restart MATLAB. Make sure that toolbox path caching is not enabled in the File > Preferences > General > Toolbox path caching pane.Reopen your model and run Simulink Design Verifier Test Generation on your model again.Locate the output directory of the Simulink Design Verifier analysis (sldv_output) and load the .mat data file that was generated by the analysis in step 2.Make sure that the MATLAB structure sldvData exists in the MATLAB workspace.Execute sldvtimer(sldvData) to understand whether timer optimization are applied during analysis.If sldvtimer(sldvData) returns 1, then disable timer optimizations by executing sldvtimer(0) and restart Simulink Design Verifier Test Generation on the model.You can get more information about sldvtimer by typing help sldvtimer.?Change log:September 14, 2011: attachment_756367_11b_win64_2011-09-14.zip: initial version, works on win64 R2011b onlySeptember 14, 2011: attachment_756367_11b_win32_2011-09-14.zip: initial version, works on win32 R2011b onlySeptember 14, 2011: attachment_756367_11b_glnxa64_2011-09-14.zip: initial version, works on glnxa64 R2011b onlySeptember 14, 2011: attachment_756367_11b_glnx86_2011-09-14.zip: initial version, works on glnx86 R2011b only??

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: For tunable parameter expression
Description: When a tunable block parameter expression contains a cast, the corresponding generated code omits the cast, which can lead to behavior that is inconsistent with simulation. For example, if the tunable block parameter expression is 1.0 - double(A > 50.0) then the generated code is 1.0 - modelP.A > 50.0) This leads to incorrect results because the minus operator has higher precedence than the comparison operator.
Workaround: When a tunable block parameter expression contains a cast, surround the operand of the cast () operator with extra parentheses.For example, if the tunable block parameter expression is1.0 - double(A > 50.0)modify this expression to be1.0 - double((A > 50.0))The generated code is1.0 - (real_T)(modelP.A > 50.0))

Summary: Nonexistent or unexpected signal bounds/ranges might be derived in Simulink Design Verifier Design Error Detection analysis
Description: Simulink Design Verifier's Design Error Detection analysis might either not derive signal bounds or ranges, or might derive unexpected signal bounds or ranges, for signals passing through virtual blocks.

Summary: Can not add Thermocouple channel on NI USB 9219 device
Description: When you add a thermocouple channel using an NI USB 9219 device, the following error occurs s = daq.createSession('ni')  s.addAnalogInputChannel('Dev6',0,'Thermocouple')  NI Error -201208:Sample rate specified is too fast for the ADC Timing Mode selected for this device.Decrease the sample rate or use a faster ADC Timing Mode.Conflicting PropertiesProperty: DAQmx_AI_ADCTimingModeCorresponding Value: DAQmx_Val_HighSpeedProperty: DAQmx_SampClk_RateCorresponding Value: 100.000000?Task Name: _unnamedTask<36>Status Code: -201208
Workaround: To workaround this issue, refer to the device specifications and set the session's rate to the rate supported by the device. Then add the thermocouple channels. NI USB 9219 supports rate between 0.1 and 50 samples per second.s = daq.createSession('ni')s.Rate = 50;s.addAnalogInputChannel('Dev6',0,'Thermocouple')?

Summary: Incorrect error message issued when referencing a triggered model
Description: Simulink issues an incorrect error message when handling a reference to a model that meets all of the following conditions : Has a root level trigger port with the Trigger type  parameter set to rising , falling  or either  Has a root level trigger port with the Triger signal sample time  parameter set to -1  Uses a variable-step solver  The incorrect error is similar to the following: Root Inport 'refModel/In1' has a sample time of [0, 0] which does not match the sample time [0, 1] of its destination: input port 1 of block 'refModel/Sum'. Root inports must have the same sample time as their destinations for model reference. Please correct this situation by changing one of the two sample times or inserting a Rate Transition block ?
Workaround: On the root level trigger port, set the Trigger signal sample time  to 0, or to any discrete sample time.

Summary: The Rotor flux position when theta = 0 parameter of the Permanent Magnet Synchronous Machine block does not work properly when set to Aligned with phase A axis (original Park)
Description: If you set the Rotor flux position when theta = 0  parameter of a Permanent Magnet Synchronous Machine block to Aligned with phase A axis (original Park) , the simulation will provide incorrect results if the number of pole pairs of the machine is greather than 1. When the number of pole pairs is 1, the simulation is correct.

Summary: Linear algebra functions may return slightly different but correct results for each evaluation on machines with Intel? Sandy Bridge processors.
Description: Certain linear algebra functions including eig , hess , inv , qr , qz , schur , svd , as well as the mldivide  (\ ) and mrdivide  (/ ) operators, may return slightly different results on each evaluation with the same inputs. The magnitude of this difference is close to floating point precision (1e-16 for doubles). This issue only occurs on machines with Intel Sandy Bridge or later processors that support the Advanced Vector Extension (AVX) instruction set. For example, the following code returns a small nonzero result: A = rand(34);  T1 = eig(A);  norm(T1-eig(A));  ans =   ??1.3477e-14

Summary: Code coverage with LDRA Testbed fails when Simulink.fileGenControl is used to specify a non-default location for generated files
Description: If you configure a model for SIL simulation, enable LDRA Testbed code coverage, and use Simulink.fileGenControl  to specify a non-default location for generated files, then running the SIL simulation produces an error similar to the following:Command line:C:\LDRA_Toolsuite\Contestbed.exe  codegen_db3b1e06413fd38b /32panq/dataset="Run 1 (rtwdemo_sil_topmodel_ert_rtw)"/force_exhdir="C:\MATLAB_Work\R2011b\codegen" /log-------------------------Command Line Mode started-------------------------Unable to perform requested analysisResults not present for dependant phases:Execute Instrumented Program(s)
Workaround: See workaround for bug report 752735.

Summary: Code generation delay or memory error for enumerated data type with a large number of enumeration values
Description: If a model uses an enumerated data type with a large number of enumeration values (for example, 1000), code generation for the model might significantly slow down or produce the following error: TLC Compiler encountered an OUT_OF_MEMORY condition. Attempting to clean up... The memory issue arises because code generation dynamically allocates resources for the enumeration values rather than preallocating them.

Description: Simulink Design Verifier generates errors on overflow conditions even when model parameter IntegerOverflowMsg  is disabled

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier incorrectly reports unreachable test objectives in Stateflow charts as Undecided.
Description: If the model includes a Stateflow chart with a graphical function that has model coverage objectives, and that graphical function is not called anywhere in the model, then Simulink Design Verifier Test Generation mode incorrectly reports those objectives as Undecided. Since the graphical function is not called, Simulink Design Verifier should mark all of the model coverage objectives associated with it as Proven Unsatisfiable.

Summary: imfilter yields incorrect results for linearly separable filter kernels when specified fill value is non-zero
Description: The function imfilter  produces incorrect output when the filter kernel is linearly separable and the specified fill value is non-zero.  For example, the following call to imfilter  produces incorrect output:  img = ones(100); kernel = fspecial('average', 15 ); padValue = 1; act = imfilter (img, kernel, padValue);  The expected output in this case is a 100x100 matrix containing all ones. For double precision floating point input, this bug can only be reproduced if the filter kernel is linearly separable and contains more than 48 elements. For all other input datatypes, this bug can only be reproduced if the filter kernel is linearly separable and contains more than 288 elements.

Summary: An Java exception is thrown when attempting to create a new project in a directory that contains an ampersand (&) character in the directory name.
Description: When creating a new project with the Deployment Tool, placing the project in a directory named C:\work\a & b , for example, throws a Java exception in the MATLAB command window. As a result,a new project is not created.
Workaround: There are no workarounds at this time.

Summary: Indexing into temporary array using end and the power function produces wrong results
Description: The following examples produce the wrong result or crash MATLAB:x{1} = [1 2 3 4 5]1*x{1}(1:end).^22*sum(x{1}(1:end).^2)
Workaround: Make sure values are stored in variables before indexing. For example:x{1} = [1 2 3 4 5];tmp = x{1};1*tmp(1:end).^22*sum(tmp(1:end).^2)

Summary: Using COM Interface to access the DataSource property of the ADODB Recordset object can crash MATLAB
Description: The following code fragment can result in the crash. Even if the property call succeeds, the returned object is invalid. recSet = actxserver('ADODB.Recordset');dataSrc = recSet.DataSource; % This line causes a crash  Other manifestations of this problem are: Crash when inspecting properties of the ADODB Recordset object on the command line using get  function: get(recSet)  Crash using the Inspector on the command line: inspect(recSet)  Crash double-clicking the Recordset object in the Workspace window? ?

Description: If an inactive subsystem of a Variant Subsystem block contains a Model block, Simulink Design Verifier compatibility checking fails and reports errors.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when you debug a bus-type Data Store Memory accessed in a MATLAB Function block.
Description: MATLAB crashes if you use the Simulink Debugger to watch the value of a Data Store Memory (DSM) array of buses inside a MATLAB Function block.

Summary: Misleading message in the Polyspace user interface when modifying the value of the -unit-by-unit option
Description: When editing a configuration in the project browser, changing the value of the -unit-by-unit  option can cause the following pop-up message: "Do you wish to propagate this setting to all configurations in this project?the same verification mode is required for all configurations when using -add-to-results-repository option to allow generation of consistent metrics. Enable -unit-by-unit mode for all configurations in this project?"  This message is misleading. The checkbox is useless since you are already modifying the -unit-by-unit  option. All configurations have the same values for the -unit-by-unit  option.
Workaround: Acknowledge the message and ignore it.

Summary: Justifications are lost when checks start orange, become gray, and then become orange again
Description: When you import comments from V1 to V2 results, and then later import comments from V2 to V3 results, justifications (Classification, Status, Justified) added to orange checks in V1 can be lost. This occurs when the originally justified check was orange (in V1 results), became gray in the V2 results, and then turned orange again in the V3 results.
Workaround: Open the version V3 of your results, then import justifications from the V1 version.Justifications that already exist in the V3 results will not be overwritten. Justifications lost on the orange checks will be imported from the V1 results.

Summary: A bug in R2011a prevents certain indexing expressions from calling overloaded subsref and subsasgn methods in classes that subclasses built-in classes.
Description: This bug prevents expressions that use '.' and '{}' indexing from calling the subsref  and subsasgn  methods in subclasses of built-in classes. Indexing expressions using '()' indexing are not affected. For example, the following class, foo , is a subclass of double  and defines an overloaded subsref  method: classdef foo < double       methods           function obj=foo(a)               obj@double(a);           end           function out=subsref(s,a)               disp('called subsref');               out=a.subs;           end       end end    The following statements cause an error instead of calling the subsref  method defined for class foo . f = foo; f{1}; ?
Workaround: There is no known workaround.

Summary: sound, soundsc, and audioplayer produce 'pop' sounds at the start and end of playback on Windows 7 systems
Description: On some Windows 7 systems, playing back certain types of audio can create a short 'pop' sound when using sound , soundsc , or audioplayer .For example, the following code produces a noticeable pop at the end of playback.freq = 500; fs = 44100; t = 0:1/fs:0.3; window = hann(round(0.015 * fs))';   rise   = window(1:length(window)/2);     fall   = window((length(window)/2)+1:end); middle = ones(1,(length(t) - length(window))); cos_window = [rise,middle,fall]; tone = 0.8 * sin(2*pi*freq*t); tone = tone .* cos_window; sound(tone, fs);
Workaround: Pad the input data with zeros:silence = zeros([1 floor(fs / 100)]);tone = [silence tone silence];

Summary: Type name collisions might cause thunk file build failure
Description: Thunk files create typedef  statements for standard types, such as:  typedef int int32 typedef char int8  For example, if your include file contains:  #define int8 char  then the typedef  statement causes a compile error similar to:  Building mylibrary_thunk_pcwin64 failed. Compiler output is: cl -I"C:\MATLAB\R2011a\extern\include" /Zp8 /W3 /D_CRT_SECURE_NO_DEPRECATE /D_SCL_SECURE_NO_DEPRECATE /D_SECURE_SCL=0 /nologo -I"l:\1-F7CDJ0" -I"l:\1-F7CDJ0" "mylibrary_thunk_pcwin64.c" -LD -Fe"mylibrary_thunk_pcwin64.dll" mylibrary_thunk_pcwin64.c mylibrary_thunk_pcwin64.c(40) : error C2632: 'char' followed by 'char' is illegal mylibrary_thunk_pcwin64.c(40) : warning C4091: 'typedef ' : ignored on left of 'char' when no variable is declared
Workaround: To work around this problem, try one of the following: Remove the offending line from the user header. Use the thunkfilename option to loadlibrary, fix the generated thunk file, and manually recompile. Patch prototypes.pl to emit guards around the typedef statements.

Summary: "Incorrect arguments" build error when using code replacement table created using Code Replacement Tool

In the Build Information  tab of crtool , you used the File  button to add an additional link object file for a table entry. You incorporated the resulting code replacement table into a registered TFL, and selected that TFL for your model.  Under these conditions, building the model might produce the following error: Incorrect arguments have been passed to: RTW.BuildInfo.addLinkObjects Please refer to the help on this method for more information. The build error occurs because crtool  generated incorrect MATLAB code for the AdditionalLinkObjs  and AdditionalLinkObjsPaths  properties of the code replacement table entry.

Summary: Polarity of capacitors and inductors cannot be visually determined
Description: The polarity of capacitor and inductor blocks in SimPowerSystems is not identified on the block icon. Consequently it is not clear how the polarity of an initial voltage or current is applied to the block. Also it is not clear how to determine the polarity of a measured current or voltage.
Workaround: To know the polarity of capacitor or inductor blocks you need to find out the block's orientation, using the following steps:1. Select the block where you want to know the polarity.2. At the MATLAB prompt, type:get_param(gcb,'Orientation') If the orientation of the block is:right - the positive terminal is on the left of the block.left - the positive terminal is on the right of the block.down - the positive terminal is on the top of the block.up - the positive terminal is on the bottom of the block.

Summary: MATLAB Function block accessing global data store from referenced model may cause simulation error
Description: Simulink may generate a simulation error if all the following conditions are met: Your model references another model whose Simulation mode  is set to Accelerator . The referenced model contains a MATLAB Function block. The MATLAB Function block reads from or writes to a global data store.  The error message has the following form: MATLAB Function Interface Error: Block 'sub/MATLAB Function/ SFunction ' is calling ssReadFromDataStoreElement with an element index of (-1); element indices must be non-negative  In some cases, the error may be invalid. You can determine whether the errror is invalid as follows: Set the Simulation mode  of the referenced model to Normal . Simulate the parent model. If the error does not appear, it was invalid.
Workaround: Restructure the algorithm in the MATLAB Function block if the following two conditions are met:The MATLAB Function block references the global data store through a condition in an if statementThe condition utilizes a short circuit operator such as && or ||Restructure the algorithm as follows:Replace && operators with nested if statements.Replace || operators with duplicate code.To prevent optimizations that might cause the original error, replace the second argument for the replaced && or || operator with a function that cannot be inlined.For example, consider the following MATLAB algorithm, where X and Y are global data store memory blocks. This algorithm utilizes the && operator.if (X > 0) && (Y(X) < 3)  k = k + 1;endYou can restructure this algorithm by using nested if statements and a noninlined functionif (X > 0)  if Y_X_less_than_3()    k = k + 1;  endend...function result = Y_X_less_than_3()  coder.inline('never') %% don't inline  result = Y(X) < 3;endSimilarly, consider the following MATLAB algorithm, which utilizes the || operatorif (X > 0) || (Y < 7)  k = k + 1;endYou can restructure this algorithm using duplicate codeif (X > 1)  k = k + 1;else if Y_less_than_7()  k = k + 1;end...function result = Y_less_than_7()  coder.inline('never') %% don't inline  result = Y < 7;endAlternatively, you can avoid duplicating a large amount of code by using Boolean conditions.guard = false;  if (X > 1)    guard = true;  else if Y_less_than_7()    guard = true;  end  if guard    k = k + 1;  end...function result = Y_less_than_7()  coder.inline('never') %% don't inline  result = Y < 7;end

Summary: Back to model command does not work
Description: Suppose you do the following: Perform a Polyspace verification on code generated using IBM Rational Rhapsody. View verification results in the Run-Time Checks perspective of the Polyspace verification environment. In the Source  pane, right-click an error. From the context menu, select Back to model . Summary: When using 2nd-level typedef types in the option "-boolean-types" Polyspace raises incorrect MISRA rule 13.2 warnings
Description: Launching a verification using the MISRA-C rule checker options '-misra2 all-rules ' and '-boolean-types bool_t,boolean_t ' on the code below, Polyspace raises an incorrect MISRA 13.2 warning: #include <assert.h>  typedef signed char int8_t;  typedef int8_t bool_t;  typedef signed char boolean_t;  int main(void) {   volatile int8_t rd;   bool_t b1 = rd;   boolean_t b2 = rd;   if ( b1 ) assert(1); /* raise a MISRA 13.2 warning */   if ( b2 ) assert(1);   return 0;  }  This occurs because the -boolean-types  option works only on 1st-level typedef.

Summary: Link to Release Notes in the Polyspace help page does not work
Description: When opening Help on a Windows system, the Polyspace help page displays links to PDF files in the right column, including a link to the Release Notes. Clicking these links does not open the corresponding PDF files.
Workaround: You can open the PDF files directly from the installation folder %POLYSPACE_COMMON%\R2011b\help\pdf. The Release Notes is named 'rn.pdf' in this folder.

Summary: Existing test cases are ignored when using the CombinedObjectives (Nonlinear Extended) strategy.
Description: Existing test cases provided by the user are ignored when generating test cases using the CombinedObjectives (Nonlinear Extended) strategy. CombinedObjectives (Nonlinear Extended) is a Test suite optimization  configuration parameter option.

Summary: Uncompilable code caused by excess elements in struct initializer for constant block outputs with variant subsystems
Description: If a variant subsystem is configured to generate preprocessor conditionals, and the variants inside that subsystem generate reusable functions, the code generated for the parent model may fail to compile due to excess elements in the initializer for the constant block output structure. The error reported by the compiler may be similar to the following:  model_data.c:38: error: extra brace group at end of initializer model_data.c:38: error: (near initialization for 'model_ConstB') model_data.c:41: warning: excess elements in struct initializer model_data.c:41: warning: (near initialization for 'model_ConstB')
Workaround: Disable code reuse for variants of subsystems configured to generate preprocessor conditionals, by setting the parameter 'Function packaging' to 'Inline' or 'Function'.

Summary: Comments added to gray checks (other than UNR) are lost after comment import
Description: With R2011b results, all gray checks are now in the UNR category (other gray checks no longer exist). When you import results from R2011a (or older), you would expect to see any gray checks you reviewed in the import report, because they no longer exist. However, these checks do not appear in this report.

Summary: HDL code generation fails for Product Block with vector inputs
Description: HDL code generation for the Product Block may fail with this error:Compilation errors when generating code for: (blockpath)/ProductError during MATLAB code compilation:Error Path: toolbox/hdlcoder/hdlcommon/emlauthoring/arithmeticops/hdleml_product.pError Location: (@Row: --> 1 @Column: --> 1)Matrix dimensions must agree.Internal Error: This error occurred inside a MathWorks function.  This error occurs when multiplying a row vector with an unordered vector.Simulink displays the dimensions of a row vector as [nxm], e.g. [1x16], and displays the dimensions of an unordered vector as [n], e.g. [16].
Workaround: Change the input vector dimensions so that they match.  A matched pair will generate the correct HDL code.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink Coder can sporadically generate zero-initialization code for bus signals and states
Description: If you initialize a signal or state with a MATLAB structure or a parameter object with a value that is a MATLAB structure, Simulink Coder might generate extra, but inconsequential, zero-initialization code for signals and states. In addition, this might have a secondary effect of generating an extra, but inconsequential, start function if the zero-initialization code occurs inside a subsystem start function.
Workaround: Set the initial condition parameter to 0 instead of using a MATLAB structure.In R2011a and earlier releases, for initializing bus signals containing elements with enumerated data types, use an initial condition structure that only specifies the fields corresponding to the elements with enumerated data types. All other fields will default to 0.

Summary: Help button does not work for blocks in the Interface Elements library
Description: If you click the Help  button in the dialog box of the following blocks: Current-Voltage Simscape Interface, Current-Voltage Simscape Interface (gnd), Voltage-Current Simscape Interface, Voltage-Current Simscape Interface (gnd), the Help Browser does not display the correct page. Instead, it opens an empty page entitled SimPowerSystems Simscape Interface Element.
Workaround: To access documentation for these blocks, open the Help Browser (or point you browser to Product Documentation at the MathWorks Web site), go to SimPowerSystems > Blocks (either By Category or Alphabetical List), and then click on the block name.

Summary: On Mac OS X 10.7 Lion, using Cmd-~ might result in a crash
Description: Using Cmd-~  to activate a window within MATLAB may subsequently cause the window to not close when clicking on its close box or using Cmd-W  to close the window, or may cause a previously closed window to reappear. Attempting to close the window a second time may result in a segmentation violation, usually with a stack trace containing [NSWindow __close] . The MathWorks has determined the underlying cause with Apple Java on OS X 10.7, and Apple has resolved this issue in Mac OS X 10.8. Users who are unable to upgrade to that version of OS X are discouraged from using Cmd-~ .

Summary: In MuPAD, implicit plots sometimes display extra dots on lines
Description: In some cases, 2-D implicit plots created by plot::Implicit2d or by plotting an equation display additional dots on the lines. For example, this command in a MuPAD notebook produces a plot with an extra dot: plot(x = y)
Workaround: Enter these commands in a MuPAD notebook:unprotect(plot):plot::Implicit2d::styleSheet[PointSize] := 0.001:protect(plot, ProtectLevelError):

Summary: Training with nnprop.grad might encounter a keyboard command
Description: Under rare numerical conditions, during training with backpropagation you might encounter a keyboard  command in nnprop.grad .
Workaround: To work around this problem, perform a dbquit and retrain. Unless the random seed is controlled, this automatically generates different weight/bias initial conditions and data division, so it results in proper training. If you encounter the problem because a script is generating hundreds or thousands of networks, this manual workaround will not work. In this case, use the grad.p  included in the attached zip file. This is a custom version of the file without the keyboard command. This function file can replace the original file at matlab/toolbox/nnet/nnutils/+nnprop/grad.p, or you can include it within a package folder in your workspace folder: users_workspace/+nnprop/grad.p.

Summary: DOORS startup errors after rmi setup in MATLAB R2011a or R2011b
Description: When running >> rmi setup  and if IBM (or Telelogic) Rational DOORS is installed, RMI installs the required DOORS API files into DOORS installation directory. DOORS versions 8.3 and earlier are vulnerable to this problem because newer version of one of the files installed by RMI has syntax that is not supported by older DOORS versions. When trying to restart DOORS after performing rmi setup, DOORS fails to start and displays an error popup: "Syntax ERROR in dmi.inc on line 490 "
Workaround: >> edit([matlabroot, '/toolbox/slvnv/reqmgt/dmi.inc'])and replace all occurrances of the following syntax:oleSetResult LS_("Message_words",NLSTEMP_("Message words"))with the following simpler syntax that is supported by old versions of DOORS:oleSetResult ("dmi.inc::localMakeMod_: Message words")For example, replaceoleSetResult LS_("String_You_must_be_in_a_project_or_folder_before_creating_or_opening_a_module",NLSTEMP_("You must be in a project or folder before creating or opening a module"))witholeSetResult ("dmi.inc::localMakeMod_: You must be in a project or folder before creating or opening a module")There is 5 occurrances in total that need to be edited.Save the file and rerun rmi setup:>> rmi setup?Attached is a patched file for R2011a. Extract under your MATLAB installation directory, quitt DOORS and rerun rmi setup?

Summary: Loading a pre-R2010b MAT-file that contains a memmapfile object fails
Description: When loading a MAT-file created in a version prior to R2010b that contains a memmapfile  object, MATLAB incorrectly sets the object properties and issues this warning:Warning: While loading an object of class 'memmapfile':When constructing an instance of class 'memmapfile', the constructor must preserve the class of the returned object.
Workaround: There are two possible workarounds.Workaround 1 (Requires two versions of MATLAB)Load the file in a version prior to R2010b and determine the properties of the object. Then, in your current version of MATLAB, create a new object with those properties. For example, if you have file myfile.mat with object myinstance, run these commands in R2010a:load myfile.matdisp(myinstance)Use the property values to create a new object in R2010b or later, ignoring the Data property. For example, if the disp command in R2010a returnedFilename: '/example/filename.txt'Writable: falseOffset: 0Format: 'uint8'Repeat: InfData: 0x1 uint8 arraythen in R2010b, use this command:myinstance = memmapfile('/example/filename.txt', ...                        'Writable', false, 'Offset', 0, ...                        'Format', 'uint8', 'Repeat', Inf);Workaround 2Edit memmapfile.m as follows. Open the file for editing:edit([matlabroot,'/toolbox/matlab/iofun/@memmapfile/memmapfile.m'])Find the function definition for loadobj:         function obj = loadobj(obj)        if isa(obj, 'memmapfile')            obj.DataHandle = 0;        end    end    Replace those lines with the following lines:    function obj = loadobj(obj)        if isa(obj, 'memmapfile')            obj.DataHandle = 0;        elseif isfield(obj, 'filename')            obj = memmapfile(obj.filename, ...                             'Writable', obj.writable, ...                             'Offset', obj.offset, ...                             'Format', obj.format, ...                             'Repeat', obj.repeat);        end    end

Summary: iradon returns reconstructed image with small offset in intensities
Description: The iradon  function filters the input projections before backprojection. Previous versions of iradon  use a perfect ramp filter for filtering, whose discrete version removes all the frequency components in the bin around the zero frequency. This leads to an offset in the reconstructed output. The new version of iradon  uses a band-limited ramp filter, which has a non-zero zero-frequency (DC) term, to filter the input projections, and this removes the offset in the reconstructed output.
Workaround: To work around this problem:1) Log into your computer using an account with Administrator privileges.2) Download the attached file, workaround_744762_10a_through_11b_2011-08-15.zip3) On Windows double-click the file to view its contents, then click the Extract button. On Macintosh, double-click the file to extract its contents. On Linux use the unzip command.4) Complete the instructions in the enclosed text file, README.txt5) The updates provided in this workaround are for releases R2010a, 2010b, 2011a, and 2011b.?

Summary: Models containing EtherCAT blocks may fail during build.
Description: A model containing EtherCAT blocks may fail during build if the model uses other blocks that also require an external library.
Workaround: In the xpcblocks folder edit the file rtwmakecfg.m.Change the line: makeInfo.library(1).Name = 'AtEcat_xpc'; To: makeInfo.library(k).Name = 'AtEcat_xpc';?

Summary: Port with enumerated type might return incorrect value or cause an assertion at build time
Description: An assertion might occur or the generated code might return an incorrect value for an enumerated type under the following conditions: A port has an enumerated type The port is unconnected or fed by a Ground block The enumerated type has a default value that is different than its position in the zero-based list of enumerated values  The default value is either the first value in the list, or if the getDefaultValue()  method is defined, the value returned by the method. The effects of using different type definitions in connection with a Ground block or an unconnected port are shown in the enumerated type definitions below. ERROR  is the default (ground) value. In the examples below, assume that the default value for the enum  type is the first in the list, ERROR . The values of ERROR  are different. The following type definition generates correct code because the value of ERROR(0)  matches its position in the list: enumeration ERROR(0) Red(1) Yellow(2) Blue(3) BONUS(10)end The following type definition produces an assertion: enumeration ERROR(-1) Red(0) Yellow(1) Blue(2) BONUS(10)end The following type definition might produce the incorrect value 2  rather than the correct value 3 . Since ERROR  is the default, the ground value should be 3 . However, in some cases, the code generator interprets the ground value as a position, and redirects to that element to compute the ground value. enumeration ERROR(3) Red(0) Yellow(1) Blue(2) BONUS(10)end
Workaround: Use a Constant block and give the constant value either of the following:The default value name. For example, Colors_Asserts.ERROR.A call to the getDefaultMethod. For example, Colors_Asserts.getDefaultMethod().To determine whether your model is affected, change the default value of each enumerated type to -1 and build the model. If there is no assertion, then there are no Ground blocks or unconnected ports that trigger the error.??

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Dead Zone block using fixed-point slope and bias data type with large bias might produce incorrect results in generated code.
Description: When using fixed-point slope and bias data types with large bias values on the Dead Zone block, Simulink Coder might generate code with incorrect numerical results. To trigger this bug, the absolute value of the bias on the slope and bias type must exceed the range of that same fixed-point data type with a zero bias. For example, feeding a signal into the Dead Zone block with the following type can trigger the bug: triggerType = fixdt(1, 23, 0.000029, 1280.52);  This is because the zero bias type: zeroBiasType = fixdt(1, 23, 0.000029, 0);  has a smaller range than the bias value of 1280.52. You can see this using the following code: fiZeroBiasType = fi(0,zeroBiasType);  fiZeroBiasType.rangeans =  -121.6348 121.6348  DataTypeMode: Fixed-point: slope and bias scaling  Signedness: Signed  WordLength: 23  Slope: 2.9e-5  Bias: 0  Detection and Verification  To detect if this bug affects your model, compare the numerical simulation results of your model against the numerical code generation results. Use inputs known to exercise the model.

Summary: bwdist gives incorrect output label matrix when input image size exceeds 224 pixels
Description: The function bwdist  may give the incorrect output label matrix (L , the second output argument) for input images with number of elements (pixels/voxels) greater than 224 . This does not happen for all such images, but only for those whose correct label matrix should contain entries (indices) with values greater than 224 . An example which results in an incorrect label matrix is shown below: img = ones(4096,5000);  [D, L] = bwdist(img);  In this case the label matrix L  will have erroneous values in the last few columns of the image.  ? ?
Workaround: Replace the function bwdist with bwdist_old in the code. bwdist_old might be relatively slower as compared to bwdist in some cases because it uses a different algorithm to compute the Euclidean distance transform.
