Summary: Simulink Design Verifier derives incorrect ranges for fixed-point data type signals in Design Error Detection analysis
Description: In the model's Configuration Parameters dialog box under the Optimization > Simulation and code generation  section, if the option "Use integer division to handle net slopes that are reciprocals of integers" is selected, Simulink Design Verifier derives incorrect ranges for fixed-point data type signals in Design Error Detection analysis.

Summary: Time-based linearization of models with Stateflow charts and Timed-Based Linerization blocks might affect simulation results
Description: Performing time-based linearization on models that contain Stateflow charts and Timed-Based Linearization blocks might alter simulation results. This issue is related to the particular logic of Stateflow charts. While not all charts are affected, you might notice the behavior when declaring and using local variables within a chart. To verify if a model that contains a Stateflow chart and Timed-Based Linearization block is affected: In the original model, configure the Timed-Based Linearization block to a set of values within the simulation time boundaries. Run the model. Save the time-domain results. Remove the Timed-Based Linearization block from the model. Run the model again. Save the time-domain results. Compare the time-domain results of the two simulations. When comparing the two results, if you observe a difference (possibly within the first time specified in the Linearization time  parameter of the Timed-Based Linearization block), your model is affected by this issue.
Workaround: As a workaround,Remove the Timed-Based Linearization block from the model.In the MATLAB Command Window, use the command linearize to obtain the linearized representation of a model at a certain point in simulation time. To do this, pass a single snapshot time as a parameter to the command linearize. Repeat this process with a different value for snapshot time to linearize the model at different simulation times (do not specify the snapshot times in vector form).This workaround enables linearization of the model without affecting its simulation output.

Summary: Simulink Scopes may cause Simulink to run out of memory on 32-bit machines.
Description: When running a Simulink model with one or more Scope blocks, your computer may run out of memory. This happens when The number of time steps in the Simulation is large (greater than 10 million time steps). Scope blocks are recording and saving simulation data to the MATLAB workspace. There is no limit to the number of data points recorded.
Workaround: You can reduce the memory needed by a Scope block with the following parameters on the Scope parameters dialog.On the General tab and from the Sampling list, select Decimation, and then set its value to a large number.On the History tab, clear the Save data to workspace check box.Select the Limit data points to last check box and enter a number.This workaround may only delay the onset of the problem, as some models will always generate enough data to cause out-of-memory errors on 32-bit machines.

Summary: C++ reserved words as field name in tunable struct parameter for MATLAB Function block causes error during code generation
Description: The use of a C++ reserved word as a field in a tunable struct parameter for a MATLAB Function block causes an internal error during code generation. The internal error occurs for C targets. If C++ is the specified target language, the Simulink Coder?? software throws the error. For example, if you have in the MATLAB command window: >> stX.new = 1; The implementation of a MATLAB function that uses the field above is: function y = fcn(stX)y = stX.new; and causes an error.
Workaround: Change the field name to a non C++ reserved words.In addition, there are a small number of names reserved by the Simulink Coder? software.

Summary: In Accelerator or Rapid Accelerator mode, simulating models with signals containing NaN values fed to indexing input ports of Selector or Assignment blocks, or executing the generated code of the model, generates a segmentation fault
Description: Running a model in Accelerator or Rapid Accelerator mode, or executing the generated code from this model, generates a segmentation fault if the following is true for the model: Contains a Selector or Assignment block whose Index  parameter is set to one of the following: Index Vector (Port)  Starting Index (Port)  Starting and Ending Indices (Port)    Has signals that contain NaN values fed to the index input of the Selector or Assignment block with the above settings

Summary: Hyperlink underlines and bounding boxes are not aligned with corresponding text
Description: On some platforms, some fonts result in Command Window hyperlinks with underlines that do not line up with the corresponding text for the link.  Additionally, the area you click to execute the link may not match the text.  This is primarily a problem with variable-width fonts, and fonts with a text size that changes for bold text.
Workaround: Set the font for the Command Window to a fixed-width font.  If you are already using a fixed-width font, experiment with another font, which may align better, particularly for bold text.To set the Command Window font:  From the MATLAB? desktop select File > Preferences > Fonts. Review the Currently used by lists for each font group to determine which one the Command Window currently uses. (By default, the Desktop code font.) From the first drop-down menu associated with the group you identified in step 2, select a font. (For example, Monospaced is a fixed-width font.)Click OK.

Summary: When Merge block inputs contain nonvirtual buses, model linearization might cause MATLAB to return wrong results or stop responding
Description: When Merge block inputs contain nonvirtual buses, model linearization might cause MATLAB to return wrong results or stop responding. This behavior is because the linearization result might have wrong answers. In some extreme cases, linearizing the model might cause MATLAB to become unresponsive.
Workaround: As a workaround, if possible, use virtual buses as input to Merge blocks.

Summary: On Windows, a zip.exe I/O error occurs when packaging files greater than 2GB with deploytool
Description: On Windows, deploytool  uses a zip executable shipped with MATALB compiler to create self-extracting archives. This verzion of Zip is not capable of processing files greater than 2 GB.

Summary: Calling static method of same class to set default value of System object property produces an error
Description: A property of a System object cannot have its default value set via a call to a static method of the same class. For example, the classdef below errors: classdef MyObject < matlab.system.System  properties    Prop = MyObject.getValue  end  methods (Static)    function val = getValue      val = 10;    end  endend  >> h = MyObjectThe class MyObject has no property or method named 'getValue'.
Workaround: To work around this error, use a local method to set the default value of the property:classdef MyObject < matlab.system.System  properties    Prop = localGetValue  end  methods (Static)    function val = getValue      val = localGetValue;    end  endendfunction val = localGetValue  val = 10;end?

Summary: Memory leak occurs when initializing and terminating a library in a loop
Description: If a deployed application is written which initializes and terminates a shared library component in a loop, the application container's heap space grows.

Summary: BERCONFIT function does not return monotonically decreasing confidence interval results for large numbers of samples
Description: The BERCONFINT function returns confidence interval values that increase as the number of samples increases for number of samples greater than 1e13. The following example presents that as the number of trials increases in order to find the 95% confidence interval, the interval gradually decreases but increases in the last three values. The following code illustrates that the interval does not steadily decrease but increases in the last 3 values in 'b'. >> format long e >> [~ b] = berconfint(zeros(10,1), 1e14*[1:10]') b = 0 2.187499999999952e-014  0 1.687499999999972e-014  0 4.791666666666644e-015  0 4.062499999999984e-015  0 2.249999999999995e-015  0 1.874999999999996e-015  0 1.607142857142855e-015  0 1.312499999999983e-014  0 1.111111111111110e-015  0 1.287499999999984e-014

Summary: Constant value block input sources can result in incorrect code.
Description: When the code generator reads constant data, the generator can access memory outside of the constant data storage. As a result, the generator may define constant values incorrectly in the generated code. This error is more likely to occur if the embedded target has no built-in 8 or 16-bit integer data types. The integer types are shown in the Configuration Parameters dialog box on the Hardware Implementation  pane. The problem has been observed on a 64-bit Windows host. ?
Workaround: Use 32-bit data types or a data type that is built-in to the hardware for constants or ground values.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Rate Limiter block generates inconsistent results between simulation and code generation under certain conditions
Description: The Rate Limiter block generates inconsistent results between simulation and code generation when the following conditions are met: The input and output are fixed-point data types. The size of the input and output data types is less than the largest integer on the target (usually size of the Long Integer). The output data type has non-zero bias.
Workaround: To work around this problem, do not use non-zero bias for the input and output data types.

Summary: Incorrect code for dynamic arrays with more than INT_MAX elements
Description: Operations on dynamic arrays larger than INT_MAX might cause incorrect indexing code due to iteration variables of type signed int . The indexing might be incorrect because of overflows in the loop's increment operation. For example, adding two such arrays or adding a scalar signal to one such array might generate the incorrect code.
Workaround: Avoid using array signals with a size larger than INT_MAX.For release R2012a and beyond an error message is thrown. You can use the following feature setting to allow code generation to proceed. Manually examine the generated code for all uses of the wide signal described above.slfeature('AllowUnsafeOversizedMatrices', 1)To re-enable the error condition:slfeature('AllowUnsafeOversizedMatrices', 0)?

Summary: Ports on some blocks are not positioned symmetrically around the center of the block
Description: In some instances, you can make changes to a block that cause its ports to no longer be arranged symmetrically around the center of the block. For blocks with a single port on a side, the port may appear off-center. In other cases, all ports on a side may be offset slightly.

Summary: Code generation fails sporadically due to file permission error on Windows platforms
Description: The code generation process may abort due to an error in file operations. When this happens, the error message reports that there is an error renaming a file because permission is denied. For example, on a Windows platform, this might happen when there is a long delay in a file operation.
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:If you see "Error: Access is denied" when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting Run as administrator.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: On UNIX platforms, loading Simulink by tab completion might cause sudden MATLAB exit
Description: Loading Simulink briefly redirects input into MATLAB from another process rather than the command line. If MATLAB attempts to ask for more work during this interval, it may erroneously diagnose that the session is reading from a file and all input is complete. MATLAB therefore exits. Because tab completion can lead to MATLAB asking for more work, the combination of loading Simulink by tab completion can produce this problem on Unix platforms. We have seen it most frequently on the Mac, but it could theoretically happen on Linux also. As an example, when Simulink has not already been loaded, type:>> Simulink. and then try a tab completion attempt. Sometimes the bug will occur.
Workaround: Load Simulink directly or by opening a model rather than by attempting to tab complete the Simulink command. After loading Simulink once for the session, tab completion is fine.

Summary: Installing Polyspace in silent mode can cause a popup message about the Visual C++ 2005 redistributable
Description: The Microsoft Visual C++ 2005 redistributable is required to use Polyspace products. When installing in silent mode, a popup window appears asking you to install the redistributable if it has not been installed.
Workaround: To workaround the issue run the following command on a cmd window:dvd-rom\Data\Windows\bin\vcredist_x86.exe /qThen start again silent install.

Summary: Import Wizard and importdata function do not always correctly import strings in text files
Description: For some text files, the Import Wizard and the importdata  function truncate fields that contain numbers and characters.  For example, given a file that contains dates and times, such as02/20/2010 07:30:45, 44.402/21/2010 08:22:33, 55.5 the Import Wizard imports this data into an array of type double :2 7 44.42 8 55.5
Workaround: When using the Import Wizard, select the appropriate column separator (such as a comma). If the Wizard shows the correct separator but not the correct data preview, first select a different separator, and then reselect the appropriate separator.When calling importdata, specify both the delimiter and the number of header lines.  For example,mydata = importdata('myfile.csv', ',', 0)

Summary: Publishing: Cannot publish multiple consecutive sections without cell breaks
Description: If you attempt to publish code that contains multiple consecutive section titles without cell breaks, the published code does not appear as expected. For example, if you publish the following code, Sections 1, 2, and 2-1 publish as separate sections, as expected. However, Section 2-2 appears as comments within Section 2-1, instead of as a separate section.  %% Test Script % Test%% Section 1% plot 1plot(rand(10,1));%% Section 2% plot 2plot(rand(15,1));%%% Section 2-1% plot 3plot(rand(20,1));%%% Section 2-2% plot 4plot(rand(25,1));

Summary: Bloomberg getdata request returns a MATLAB invalid field name error
Description: If you enter: c = blp;   getdata(c, 'AAP Equity', '5y_mid_cds_spread')   no event data is returned. The code attempts to pad the requested field with dummy data but fails, as this field name cannot be used in a MATLAB structure. The following error is returned: ??? Invalid field name: '5y_mid_cds_spread'

Summary: Cannot enter variable expressions in the Simulink Design Verifier temporal operator blocks parameter dialog boxes.
Description: You cannot enter a variable expression in the fields on the Simulink Design Verifier temporal operator blocks parameter dialog boxes. An error results.

Summary: Analog output triggers immediately when you set the TriggerType to 'HwDigital' for S-series boards
Description: Analog output triggers immediately, without waiting for the hardware trigger to occur even when you set the TriggerType to 'HwDigital' for S-series boards such as the NI PCI-6111 and NI PCI-6115.

Summary: Error occurs when acquiring analog input data using the DAQ legacy interface, SamplesPerTrigger property on NI 6221
Description: When you acquire analog input data with SamplesPerTrigger set to a large number or to Inf , and TriggerType set to HwDigital, the following errors occur: ??? NIDAQmx error : Measurements: Requested value is not a supported value for this property. Property: DAQmx_SampQuant_SampPerChan You Have Requested: 0 Valid Values Begin with: 2 Valid Values End with: 8589934590 Task Name: MWDAT0028 Status Code: -200077  Error in ==> daqdevice.start at 62 start( daqgetfield(obj,'uddobject') );  Error in ==> Repro_code at 40  ??? NIDAQmx error : The requested memory could not be allocated. Task Name:  MWDAT0034 Status Code: -50352  Error in ==> daqdevice.start at 62  start( daqgetfield(obj,'uddobject') );  Error in ==> Test_code at 53  This issue was observed using  the following devices: National Instruments NI PCI-6221 National Instruments NI USB-6221 National Instruments NI USB-6221 (BNC)
Workaround: This issue is fixed in the session-based interface triggering functions. Use the following code to use multiple triggers per run and external hardware trigger.For finite acquisitions:s = daq.createSession('ni')s.addAnalogInputChannel('Dev3',0,'Voltage')s.addTriggerConnection('External','Dev3/PFI2','StartTrigger');s.TriggersPerRun = 50;s.startForeground;For continous acquisitions:s = daq.createSession ('ni');s.addAnalogInputChannel('cDAQ1Mod1', 'ai0', 'Voltage');lh = s.addlistener('DataAvailable', @plotData); s.IsContinous = true;s.addTriggerConnection('External','Dev3/PFI2','StartTrigger');s.startBackground();

Summary: Some Japanese characters do not print correctly from the MATLAB Web browser
Description: In some situations, Japanese characters do not print correctly from the MATLAB Web browser or Help browser.
Workaround: Save the HTML file to disk using the Save As option in the File menu, open the file using your system web browser, and print the file from that browser.

Summary: Editing a stylesheet can freeze the Report Explorer
Description: Editing a stylesheet can freeze the Report Explorer. This occurs if you edit a stylesheet element and then switch to another node in the Report Explorer tree, without first saving the stylesheet.
Workaround: After you make a change to a stylesheet element, save the stylesheet before switching to another node in the Report Explorer tree.

Summary: Signal label propagation may return incorrect results when used with bus signals
Description: Signal label propagation can return incorrect results when used with bus signals. The following problems have been identified: When the Show Propagated Signals  option is set to on  for a bus signal, the results are different for virtual buses than they are for nonvirtual buses. Labeled signals do not always propagate through Bus Assignment blocks. For library blocks, the Show Propagated Signals  option may not be available for bus signals that use bus objects. When you update a diagram, some propagated signal names may change. After the update completes, if you open the model and click on a propagated signal name, the signal name that you see may differ from the name that resulted when you updated diagram. Merge, Switch, and Multiport Switch blocks incorrectly continue to display propagated bus signal labels, even when you switch the block inputs from bus signals to non-bus signals. Rate Transition block inconsistently propagates signal labels based on the data integrity option. Signal name propagation does not work when both of these conditions exist: The outputs of a conditionally executed subsystem are connected to a Merge block. The Configuration Parameters > Diagnostics > Data Validity > Underspecified initialization detection  diagnosic is set to Simplified .

Summary: Publishing: MATLAB? stops capturing output if a return statement is on the last line in a cell
Description: If the last line in a cell is a return  statement, then MATLAB? will not capture the output (such as changed figures) created by the code in that cell.

Summary: Linear algebra operations involving sparse matrices or small full matrices may see a decrease in performance on some 8-core machines running on 32-bit Windows XP.
Description: Linear algebra operations that use the BLAS library may experience a performance decrease on 8-core Intel Xeon machines running on 32-bit Windows XP operating system. This occurs only for operations on small full matrices or sparse matrices. To determine if you have this issue, run the bench  command and observe the time of the 'Sparse' task. Then use the maxNumCompThreads  command to set the maximum number of computational threads to 1, and run the bench  command again. The time of the 'Sparse' task should decrease by a factor of 5 or more if you have this issue. The following code shows this workflow: maxNumCompThreads('automatic');bench(3)maxNumCompThreads(1);bench(3)
Workaround: If you experience slow performance on any machine with 8 or more cores, experiment with decreasing the maximum number of computational threads. The maxNumCompThreads command sets the maximum number of computational threads. For example, to set the maximum number of computational threads to 4, do this:maxNumCompThreads(4);Alternatively, you can also set the KMP_BLOCKTIME environment variable to 0 before starting MATLAB?. However, setting the KMP_BLOCKTIME environment variable may have a negative performance impact for some code. Therefore, setting this variable is not generally recommended. You should only set this variable when you run programs that are affected by this issue.

Summary: On Mac OS X 10.5 (Leopard), maximizing or making a figure's size very large can cause its content to be redrawn incorrectly
Description: When running MATLAB on Mac OS X 10.5 Leopard, a figure can repaint its content incorrectly when you maximize the figure window or change the figure Position  property to enlarge the figure window. For example, the following code creates a figure with an axes and a line in it: f = figure;line;set(f,'position',[ 200 200 400 200]). Clicking the green button several times to maximize and restore the figure window results in an incorrect display. ?
Workaround: Moving, resizing, or minimizing and then restoring the figure window causes its content to redraw correctly.

Summary: Running a report on large models causes a Java out-of-memory error
Description: Running the Report Generator on large models can return an error similar to the following: Document conversion failedError using ==>rpt_xml.db_output.convertReport at 206Java exception occurred:java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: Java heapspace  The error message appears during the conversion from XML to the document format (for example, to PDF), after the Report Generator has run on the models.
Workaround: Try increasing Java heap memory for MATLAB:From the MATLAB menu bar, select File > Preferences to open the MATLAB Preferences dialog box.In the Preferences dialog box, select General > Java Heap Memory.Set the Java heap memory to the maximum available.If your report still runs out of memory, try one of these approaches:Run the report on a system with more memory. Use model referencing to modularize your model, and then create separate reports for the top model and for the referenced models.

Summary: Problem loading exponential values from an SBML file SimBiology versions before 4.1
Description: In some cases there is a problem loading values in exponential notation from SBML files. Not all values in this form exhibit the problem and it is not understood which situations trigger the problem.
Workaround: Saving the model as a SimBiology project is the only workaround. Use sbiosaveproject to save.

Summary: Simulink may issue a spurious "Signal label mismatch" warning or error for a Bus Selector block
Description: Simulink may issue a spurious warning or error message during simulation when: Configuration Parameters > Diagnostics > Connectivity > Signal label mismatch  is set to warning  or error . The model contains a Bus Selector block whose output is testpointed or provides an input signal to a Model block.  The spurious warning or error message is similar to: Signal label mismatch: 'model_name/Bus Selector' output port 1 label '<sig_name>', should match 'model_name/upstream_block' output port 1 label 'sig_name'.  If a warning message occurs, simulation continues and simulation results are unaffected.
Workaround: The simplest workaround is to set Configuration Parameters > Diagnostics > Connectivity > Signal label mismatch to none. However, this technique can be risky, because it also suppresses warning or error messages for any genuine signal label mismatches that may exist.Another possibility is to set Signal label mismatch to warning, or leave it set that way. Spurious warning messages will appear during future simulations, but they will not affect simulation results.If spurious messages result from a Bus Selector block that inputs to a Model block, you can insert a Signal Specification block at the output of the Bus Selector, and use it to assign to the output signal the same name as the corresponding input signal within the referenced model.If spurious messages result from testpointing the output of a Bus Selector block, you can remove the testpoint by opening Signal Properties for the Bus Selector output, and unchecking Test point.

Summary: Why does IMREAD error out with "too many IDATs" found while reading PNG files?
Description: Some PNG files may contain too many IDAT chunks, and the current version of the LIBPNG library will signal an error if it detects this situation. If all of the IDAT chunks that constitute a valid image have been read before arriving at any invalid chunks, it is still possible to read and return the image and any metadata.

Summary: Starting a large parallel job under the job manager can stall or fail
Description: When using a job manager to run a parallel job or start a pmode session, the job might appear to stall. This happens sporadically, but is much more common when running with more than 64 workers.In R2007a, a timeout was introduced which will cancel any parallel job when the parallel session MPI startup phase has exceeded a certain amount of time.
Workaround: This problem occurs only when running parallel jobs under the job manager. Therefore, if you can use another scheduler, you will not encounter this problem.

Summary: 64-bit Windows platforms:?MATLAB?Web browser might incorrectly display HTML files created with Microsoft Office
Description: HTML files created or edited with Microsoft Office sometimes do not display correctly in the MATLAB Web browser on 64-bit Windows platforms.?Depending on the HTML content, the page might display correctly, or it might?be blank or unreadable.
Workaround: Use the web function with the -browser option to display the file in your system browser.

Summary: Large scientific notation values may not be preserved during SBML import-export
Description: When you import SBML files into SimBiology, large exponential values are sometimes imported in non-exponential form (for example, 7e8 may be imported as 700000000). When you export a model that has large numbers in formulas, overflow can occur, resulting in incorrect data in the SBML file.

Summary: Print Dialog Box Properties Button is Inactive
Description: When you select File -> Print  or Print Selection  from some desktop components, the Properties  button in the Print dialog box is not selectable. Specifically, this applies to printing from the Editor/Debugger, Command Window, Command History, Workspace browser, and Array Editor. This is a known limitation of Java, and Sun has an open Request For Enhancement to support the Properties  button.
Workaround: You can copy and paste selected text into another application that  allows you to specify print properties, and then print from that application.Alternatively, print to a PDF creator and then use a PDF viewer to print as desired
